riskGauges.note: No injury history. Strong upward trajectory at 17. Starting for a senior loan club, producing elite numbers in creation and defensive contribution simultaneously. The finishing and decision consistency are genuine questions, but both are coachable with the right environment.

riskGauges.label: Psychological
riskGauges.note: Bravery, defensive engagement, and ownership in attacking phases at 17 are all positive signals. Involvement drifts in slower phases — a concentration habit to correct, not a character concern. No off-field flags.

riskGauges.label: Market
riskGauges.note: €1M for this profile is not a reflection of the output — it is a reflection of how under-scouted this market is. Unrepresented, contracted until 2028, owned by Red Star. The valuation gap between current price and where this goes in 12 months is notable.

riskGauges.label: Systemic
riskGauges.note: A clearly preferred environment — transition-heavy, open games, wide isolation opportunities. The output drops in controlled positional systems, but the core attributes (dribbling, pressing, creation) are not system-dependent. A slight drop outside ideal conditions, not a cliff edge.

players.trajectoryNote: An 8 projects as a consistent starter at a top-division club contributing directly through creation, pressing, and duel-winning simultaneously. If the finishing catches the creative output, the number moves.

verdict.statement: A 17-year-old right winger on loan from Red Star who is already producing the most complete forward profile in the 25–26 SuperLiga dataset — elite on creation, elite on defensive contribution, simultaneously, in the same matches. The finishing is the gap between the current rating and a higher one. The decision hierarchy needs work. The physical duel resistance is still developing. None of those things change what the dual output already represents: a profile that most wingers at any age in this league do not produce. The ceiling depends on whether the end-product catches up to the setup. The floor is already high.
travels: First-step acceleration and 1v1 separation — genuine across phases, not system-produced
Elite creation output — 99th-percentile shot assists and 97th-percentile xA at 17, confirmed by the assist chain across all three levels
Defensive contribution — 96th-percentile pAdj Tkl+Int for a winger; active pressing instinct, not positional proximity
Contact dribbling under pressure — 91st-percentile success rate built on balance and direction change, not burst
Vertical run timing and half-space reads — consistent across match contexts
targeted: Finishing efficiency — 54th-percentile goals against 83rd-percentile shot quality; the positions are good, the conversion is not yet matching them
Final-third decision hierarchy — dribble, shoot, or pass chosen without consistent logic; the creative ceiling requires a clearer trigger
Dribbling against patient defenders — contact style works when opponents commit; narrows when they wait and force him wide
Physical duel resistance — upper-body still developing; can be displaced in sustained shoulder-to-shoulder contact
verdict.potentialProjection: An 8 projects as a consistent starter at a top-division club contributing directly through creation, pressing, and duel-winning in the same role simultaneously. The athletic base and dual contribution profile are strong enough to scale. If the finishing catches the creative output, the number moves.

players.verdictLine: A technically advanced right half-space playmaker whose value is built on ball-carrying, set-piece quality, and genuine goal threat from midfield. The short passing and defensive discipline are the gaps. A possession-oriented club buying him now is buying a future top-division starter at a price the market has not yet recalibrated to the output.

description: Primarily a right central midfielder who gravitates toward the right half-space, where he receives between the lines, drives forward, and either shoots or unlocks runners.
Left-footed on the right side creates the same inside-cutting dynamic as an inverted winger, but from deeper.
The ball-carrying volume is extreme; the forward passing volume is not. He moves the game forward primarily with his body rather than his distribution.

onBall.title: Receives between the lines facing goal
onBall.text: Consistently positions to take the ball on the half-turn. The first touch sets up the next action rather than just securing possession — he is already deciding where the play goes when the ball arrives.

onBall.title: Drives forward rather than passing forward
onBall.text: 100th-percentile progressive runs, 58th-percentile progressive passes. When the opportunity to advance exists, the default is to carry, not to play. Combines body control and close touch to escape pressure in tight areas rather than relying on explosive separation.

onBall.title: Unlocks play with disguised, incisive distribution
onBall.text: Delivers through balls, diagonal switches, and weighted slips into runners. When he does pass forward, the decision is usually sharp. 92nd-percentile xA and 90th-percentile shot assists confirm the quality when it arrives.

onBall.title: High-volume, range-confident shooter
onBall.text: 95th percentile in shots per 90. Generates power and accuracy from mid-range with clean mechanics. Dangerous from direct free kicks. The volume and goal output are elite; the positions those shots come from are not.

onBall.title: Short passing is the weak link
onBall.text: 24th-percentile short and medium pass accuracy against 98th-percentile long pass accuracy. Ball circulation in tight areas breaks down when the next action is a short combination rather than a carry or a longer ball.

offBall.title: Connects zones rather than staying fixed
offBall.text: Drops to offer passing angles in deeper phases, then advances quickly to be available in the final third. Not a positional player in the traditional sense — the zone he occupies changes with the game state.

offBall.title: Attacks space selectively
offBall.text: Drives forward when gaps open but prefers to receive to feet over repeated runs in behind. The carrying profile is about exploiting space he finds, not space he manufactures through movement.

offBall.title: Willing but unstructured defensively
offBall.text: Engages in pressing phases and tracks back. 86th percentile in fouls committed and 95th in cards signal aggression without discipline — competing without always knowing when to stop competing.

offBall.title: More decisive with freedom than structure
offBall.text: Output increases when allowed to receive facing play with room to drive. Compact, high-intensity defensive blocks reduce separation opportunity and limit his involvement. System-freedom is a clear performance variable.

pure.name: Ball-Carrying
pure.desc: 100th-percentile progressive runs in the 1. HNL. The elite tool — built on agility and coordination, not pace.

pure.name: Long-Range Passing
pure.desc: 98th-percentile long pass accuracy. Switches play, finds runners over distance, and delivers on set pieces with rare precision.

pure.name: Goal Threat from Midfield
pure.desc: 98th-percentile non-penalty goals at 20 in senior football. The volume, conviction, and technique is a real skill.

context.name: Creation Output
context.desc: 92nd-percentile xA and 90th-percentile shot assists. When he plays the final ball, it arrives with genuine danger.

context.name: Half-Space Intelligence
context.desc: Finds the right half-space pockets consistently and adjusts positioning to stay available across zones. The between-lines positioning is a formed habit.

system.name: Freedom Dependency
system.desc: Output increases significantly in possession-oriented systems that give advanced midfielders rotational freedom. Rigid pressing structures limit involvement.

system.name: Set-Piece Threat
system.desc: Dangerous from dead balls — direct shots, deliveries, and long throws add secondary value to every set-piece phase.

exposed.name: Short Passing
exposed.desc: 24th-percentile short and medium pass accuracy. The single most important technical gap for progression into possession-based systems.

exposed.name: Defensive Duels
exposed.desc: 29th-percentile duels won, 10th-percentile aerial. Structurally exposed in transition without a protecting pivot underneath him.

exposed.name: Discipline
exposed.desc: 95th-percentile cards at 20 is unsustainable at higher levels with tighter referee standards and accumulated suspension risk.

athletic.label: Agility
athletic.value: The primary athletic tool. Strong balance and clean mechanics in tight areas. The 100th-percentile carrying is built on agility and coordination rather than pace.

athletic.label: Acceleration
athletic.value: Moderate initial burst. Adequate once in motion. Not an explosive first-step player — the separation in carrying comes from timing and body movement, not speed.

athletic.label: Physical frame
athletic.value: Needs further strength development for higher-intensity central midfield duels. 29th-percentile duels won reflects both the current physical ceiling and a style that avoids extended contact.

athletic.label: Aerial
athletic.value: 10th percentile. A genuine weakness — not a positional issue, not a sample-size concern. Will be targeted in physical leagues.

cognitive.label: Spatial reading
cognitive.value: Advanced understanding of tempo and passing angles. Finds the right half-space pockets consistently and adjusts positioning to stay available across zones.

cognitive.label: Decision speed
cognitive.value: Controlled rather than rushed. Prioritises clarity over pace — which produces composed play but occasionally slows attacks when a quick combination window exists.

cognitive.label: Carry vs. pass selection
cognitive.value: 100th-percentile carrying alongside 24th-percentile short pass accuracy suggests he may be over-carrying in situations where the short pass is the correct choice. The key developmental question.

cognitive.label: Adaptability
cognitive.value: Best when granted creative freedom. Positional rigidity in structured systems reduces involvement — the tactical demands of the next level will require more disciplined positioning.

psychological.label: Confidence
psychological.value: Comfortable taking responsibility in decisive moments. The shooting volume and willingness to carry from deep both reflect a player who seeks the ball in difficult situations.

psychological.label: Composure
psychological.value: Calm under pressure on the ball. Capable of dictating rhythm in possession phases. Technical composure is ahead of physical composure in duels.

psychological.label: Discipline
psychological.value: 95th-percentile cards. The aggression is real and directional — he competes — but the inability to stop competing at the right moment is a problem at the next level.

psychological.label: Set-piece mentality
psychological.value: Dangerous from dead balls — direct shots, deliveries, and long throws add secondary value. The set-piece output can inflate contribution metrics relative to open-play influence.

devPriorities.title: Short passing reliability
devPriorities.text: 24th-percentile short and medium pass accuracy is the single most important technical gap. Ball circulation breaks down specifically in the short-combination moments that define possession-based play at higher levels.

devPriorities.title: Carry vs. pass decision calibration
devPriorities.text: Whether the elite carrying rate reflects intelligence or avoidance of unreliable short passing is the most important unanswered question. As the short passing improves, the decision calibration should shift naturally.

devPriorities.title: Defensive positional discipline
devPriorities.text: The willingness to press and compete is genuine. The shape awareness during pressing phases and the positioning off the ball in defensive structures is not yet reliable. This is a tactical education task — the effort is already there.

devPriorities.title: Physical robustness for central midfield duels
devPriorities.text: The 29th-percentile duel win rate and 10th-percentile aerial success are genuine exposure points. Upper-body strength development and body contact technique are the practical levers.

prose: The ball control, first touch, spatial reading, and long-range technical quality are not products of the 1. HNL environment — they would show at a higher level.

The 7.0 reflects the honest gaps. Short passing accuracy at the 24th percentile is a structural weakness in possession-based systems. The contract situation — expiring June 2026 — adds time pressure but also creates a market opportunity.

The best immediate environment is possession-oriented, gives advanced midfielders rotational freedom, and has a protecting double-pivot underneath him. La Liga or Serie A structures would accelerate the technical development.

leagueScores.note: Physical load and transition intensity would test him immediately. Without explosiveness and short-passing reliability, sustained influence would fluctuate.
The best current fit. Possession orientation, technical emphasis, and rotational freedom for advanced midfielders align precisely with his profile.
Verticality helps in open games. Transition speed reduces margin for error in tight moments. Moments of real quality but consistency requires physical development.
Compact structures suit his spatial reading and between-lines comfort. Can operate here; development would require added strength.
Athletic duels are frequent and the physicality can be unforgiving. Repeated contact situations would challenge him over time.
Space and technical emphasis allow him to dictate phases and grow into games. A clean developmental bridge.
Physical demands test the frame without the transition intensity of Germany or France. System-dependent more than league-dependent here.

riskGauges.label: Development
riskGauges.note: 1,876 senior minutes at 20, consistent starter, upward trajectory season on season. The short-passing and defensive discipline gaps are coachable. No injury history.

riskGauges.label: Psychological
riskGauges.note: Confident, composed on the ball, takes responsibility in decisive moments. Card accumulation reflects competitive instinct rather than attitude problems.

riskGauges.label: Market
riskGauges.note: €1.5M with a contract expiring June 2026 creates urgency on both sides. The valuation is reasonable but the contract situation creates a bidding window that closes quickly.

riskGauges.label: Systemic
riskGauges.note: Output is clearly higher in possession-oriented, rotationally free systems. A club that acquires him for the wrong system will not see the profile the data describes.

players.trajectoryNote: Projects as a consistent starter in a possession-oriented top-division system. The ceiling moves toward 8 if the short-passing gap closes. It stays at 7.5 if it does not.

verdict.statement: A technically advanced right half-space playmaker whose value is built on ball-carrying, set-piece quality, and genuine goal threat from midfield. The profile is real and the output is consistent. The short passing and defensive discipline are the gaps that separate the current ceiling from a higher one. The contract situation in June 2026 creates a window that is not permanent. A possession-oriented club that acquires him now, builds the right protective structure behind him, and addresses the short-passing gap directly is buying a future top-division starter at a price the market has not yet recalibrated to the output.
travels: Ball-carrying quality — 100th-percentile progressive runs built on agility and coordination, not speed-dependent
Creation output — 92nd-percentile xA and 90th-percentile shot assists; the forward passing is elite when it arrives
Goal threat from midfield — 98th-percentile goals at 20 in senior football, with set-piece danger adding secondary value
Long passing range — 98th-percentile long pass accuracy; switches play and delivers over distance with rare precision
Spatial reading and half-space occupation — consistently finds the right pockets, functional across multiple structures
targeted: Short passing accuracy — 24th percentile; the single most important technical gap for progression into possession-based systems
Defensive positioning and duel resistance — 29th-percentile duels won, 10th-percentile aerial; structurally exposed in transition without a protecting pivot
Disciplinary record — 95th-percentile cards at 20 is unsustainable at higher levels with tighter referee standards
System dependency — output drops measurably in rigid structures that require quick short combinations
verdict.potentialProjection: Projects as a consistent starter in a possession-oriented top-division system — a midfielder who contributes through carrying, creation, set-piece threat, and goal scoring simultaneously. The ceiling moves toward 8 if the short-passing gap closes.

players.verdictLine: A 20-year-old at Ferencváros who already controls the tempo of games from central midfield, generates chances at an elite rate in a competitive comparison pool, and has nine senior international caps before turning 21. The aerial limitation is real and will not disappear. Everything else is either already at the level needed or trending in the right direction.

description: Operates in the right central channel of Ferencváros's midfield, dropping between lines to receive and immediately turning play forward. The underlying habit is consistent across every role he occupies: receive on the half-turn, face play, look for the forward option first.

Takes corners and direct free-kicks for Ferencváros — the delivery quality is ready-made, not in development. Comfortable at no. 8 and no. 10; asks more of him structurally as a deeper creator in a double pivot.

onBall.title: Half-turn receiver who faces play before the ball arrives
onBall.text: Drops consistently between lines to offer an outlet, and receives already turning. The first touch sets up the next action rather than protecting possession. He is deciding where the play goes as the ball leaves the passer's foot, not after it lands at his feet.

onBall.title: Short-to-medium range is his primary progression tool
onBall.text: Prefers vertical passes of 10 to 25 metres over switches or long diagonals. The 88th-percentile short and medium pass completion does not reflect conservative choices — it reflects precise ones. He plays forward and makes it stick.

onBall.title: Selective with the long ball
onBall.text: Attempts the diagonal only when a clear overload is visible. Long pass completion in the 28th percentile tells you the sample is small and the attempts are ambitious rather than speculative.

onBall.title: Shields and retains under pressure on his right side
onBall.text: Uses frame and arms to hold off contact while protecting the ball. Under a press he invites the pressure and plays through it rather than retreating. The 100th-percentile progressive run rate reflects how consistently this results in forward ball movement.

onBall.title: Set-piece delivery is a deployable weapon
onBall.text: Takes corners and direct free-kicks for Ferencváros. The delivery quality holds up on film and transfers immediately. A buying club does not need to develop this — it is available from day one.

offBall.title: Press trigger with fast reaction speed
offBall.text: Steps forward to close ball-carriers in the middle third, forcing play wide before the build-up develops. The pressing is trigger-based and reactive rather than systematic.

offBall.title: Lateral roamer who holds connection to the shape
offBall.text: Shifts across midfield to support the ball-near teammate and provide a passing link. The 28th-percentile pAdj tackles and interceptions reflects this positional contribution style. He defends through presence and proximity rather than through aggressive ball-winning volume.

offBall.title: Bait and release in tight central areas
offBall.text: Draws pressure onto himself deliberately, then uses one-touch play to exploit the space created. The 96th-percentile shot assists are the data signature of this pattern. He does not just retain the ball under pressure — he uses the pressure to generate chances for others.

offBall.title: Aerial limitation is confirmed and structural
offBall.text: Competes in aerial duels at the 17th percentile. The issue is timing and aggression rather than frame — he is 1.81m and 20, meaning the physical platform is largely set. A buying club manages this permanently through set-piece structure, not through development investment.

offBall.title: Ground duel engagement is selective but effective when committed
offBall.text: The 43rd-percentile overall duel win rate sits alongside a 96th-percentile defensive duel win rate — he does not seek duels out, but completes them at an elite rate when he does.

pure.name: Pre-receive scanning
pure.desc: Decisions are formed before the pressure begins. The speed he appears to play at is partly a function of this — built in, not coached, and not adjustable by a tighter press.

pure.name: Chance creation quality
pure.desc: 100th-percentile xA, 96th-percentile shot assists from a comparison pool that includes players with European experience. The quality is real against a competitive group, not a soft one.

pure.name: Progressive run engine
pure.desc: 100th percentile. Constant forward availability as a function of directional habit, not sprinting ability.

context.name: Set-piece delivery
context.desc: Takes corners and direct free-kicks for Ferencváros. A deployable weapon from day one at a new club — no development timeline required.

context.name: Press resistance
context.desc: Ball retention in tight central areas already tested in Europa League football. He invites pressure and plays through it rather than retreating.

system.name: Possession dependency
system.desc: Creation output requires possession context and a protecting pivot. A direct team without that structure will not see the profile this data describes.

system.name: Tempo reading
system.desc: Reads momentum shifts early and adjusts his passing pace accordingly. Speeds the game up when space opens and slows it deliberately when structure is needed.

exposed.name: Aerial duels
exposed.desc: 17th percentile. Structural, not developmental. Set-piece defensive planning must account for this before signing, not after.

exposed.name: Dribble success under pressure
exposed.desc: 26th percentile. When passing lanes close, the ball can go with them. Surfaces more often in a better league.

exposed.name: Progressive pass frequency
exposed.desc: 50th percentile in attempts despite 100th-percentile quality from those attempts. The habit of attempting what he is capable of has to match the quality already present.

athletic.label: Frame
athletic.value: 1.81m, proportionate. Holds ground in duels well enough that physicality is not a structural concern at the next level. The aerial gap is a timing and aggression problem, not a height one.

athletic.label: Engine
athletic.value: Box-to-box availability sustained across 90 minutes. The 100th-percentile progressive run rate comes from constant forward movement, not explosive pace. No injury history across a full competitive season.

athletic.label: Ground duels
athletic.value: 43rd percentile overall, 96th when committed. He selects his engagements and converts them at an elite rate. Systems that require high-volume defensive dueling will find a mismatch; systems that provide a protecting pivot will not.

athletic.label: Physical ceiling
athletic.value: The frame is largely set at 20. What you see now is approximately what transfers — an adequate physical profile for a no. 8 or no. 10, as long as the system accounts for the aerial gap at set pieces.

cognitive.label: Pre-receive scanning
cognitive.value: The defining habit. Decisions are formed before the pressure begins. This is built in, not adjustable by a tighter press.

cognitive.label: Tempo reading
cognitive.value: Reads momentum shifts early and adjusts his passing pace accordingly. At 20, this is an unusual trait. It is already present, not arriving through coaching.

cognitive.label: Risk calibration
cognitive.value: Conservative with possession risk, proactive with physical risk. Takes the line-breaking ball when the overload is real, not theoretical.

cognitive.label: Progressive pass frequency
cognitive.value: 50th percentile in progressive pass attempts against 100th in expected assists from those attempts. The quality exists. The habit of attempting it at the rate the quality warrants is the most important developmental question.

psychological.label: Composure under pressure
psychological.value: Rare for his age, supported by actual evidence rather than projection. Stable reaction to errors. Adapts mid-match when the system shifts. The Europa League has provided a real pressure environment, and nothing visible has broken down under it.

psychological.label: International trust
psychological.value: Nine senior Hungary caps at 20. A national coaching staff does not award those minutes to potential — they award them to reliability.

psychological.label: Risk appetite balance
psychological.value: Conservative with the ball, proactive off it. Produces clean possession and competitive off-ball engagement simultaneously rather than trading one for the other.

psychological.label: Off-field profile
psychological.value: No injury history. No reported concerns. The profile reads cleanly, and at €2.50M there is almost no downside protection required.

devPriorities.title: Progressive pass frequency relative to quality
devPriorities.text: He sits at the 50th percentile for attempts and the 100th for quality from those attempts. At a higher level, windows open and close faster. The scanning already supports more attempts — the question is whether the confidence to act on what he sees follows the move up.

devPriorities.title: Carrying under pressure when passing options disappear
devPriorities.text: 26th-percentile dribble success is the gap between his normal ball retention and what happens when the passing lane closes. He rarely dribbles, mostly correctly, but when forced into it the success rate drops. It surfaces more often at the next level.

devPriorities.title: Aerial defending at set pieces — managed structurally
devPriorities.text: 17th-percentile aerial duel win rate reflects timing and aggression rather than frame. The right response at any buying club is to assign a defensive header partner and position him accordingly. Development investment here is not well spent.

prose: The 9 is justified as much by the absence of risk as by the presence of elite traits. No injury history. No off-field concerns. Europa League minutes at 19. Senior international football at 20. The scanning, tempo reading, and press resistance are cognitive and technical habits — they survive environmental change because they formed before any specific environment shaped them.

The one point withheld is honest. The aerial limitation requires structural management at any level, and the dribble success rate will be tested harder in a better league. Neither is a ceiling constraint. Both require a plan before the acquisition, not after.

leagueScores.note: A mid-table technical club — Brentford, Bournemouth, Brighton — provides the possession structure and protecting pivot he needs while the physical ceiling develops. The aerial gap requires set-piece management but does not prevent weekly involvement.

Cognitive and technical demands suit his profile well. The lower average tempo relative to Germany allows his scanning and playmaking to operate without the immediate intensity that most exposes the aerial and duel weaknesses.
High tempo and transition volume align well with his press resistance and scanning quality. Viable between 6th and 12th in the table with a structured double pivot behind him.

Tactical discipline is a genuine fit. Compact defensive spaces will expose the aerial limitation more than other leagues, and Italy's shift toward more physically direct central midfield play adds a demand he is not built for right now.

Athletic and physically chaotic midfields will expose his limitations more consistently than Germany or Spain. His strengths require a structural predictability that Ligue 1's variance does not reliably provide.

High-possession, structured environments maximise his scanning, composure, and progressive passing while keeping the aerial and duel exposure manageable. Ajax, AZ, and PSV carry structures that unlock what he does. The strongest immediate fit.

Technically demanding enough to develop him further, physically manageable for his current profile. Club Brugge and Anderlecht carry possession-based structures that suit his playmaking. A legitimate first-step alternative to the Eredivisie.

riskGauges.label: Development
riskGauges.note: Regular starter at the top Hungarian club, Europa League minutes, nine senior international caps at 20. Clear upward trajectory in the data from 24-25 to 25-26. No injury history.

riskGauges.label: Psychological
riskGauges.note: No off-field concerns. Mature beyond his age by observable evidence rather than projection. Self-regulating under pressure. The psychological case for this move is as clean as any profile at this price point.

riskGauges.label: Market
riskGauges.note: €2.50M for a player the CIES Football Observatory ranked first among all under-20 chance-providing midfielders across 60 leagues globally in the 2025–26 season. The 2027 contract expiry concentrates the timeline.

riskGauges.label: Systemic
riskGauges.note: Creation output drops outside possession-oriented systems, and the defensive exposure widens without a protecting pivot. Not system-dependent to a dangerous degree, but the coaching environment is a meaningful variable.

players.trajectoryNote: Projects as a consistent starter and genuine creative influence at a top-five European club. Contributes at Champions League level as a playmaker and chance-creator. The ceiling above 8 moves if the progressive pass frequency normalises to match the quality already present.

verdict.statement: A 20-year-old who already has the two things you cannot coach into a midfielder: the habit of scanning before the ball arrives, and the ability to read when the game needs speeding up versus slowing down. The creation output is confirmed in European football, not projected from domestic data alone. The aerial gap is permanent and manageable. The dribble gap and progressive pass frequency are both trending in the right direction. A possession-oriented club that builds the right structure around him is making an accurate bet.
travels: Elite chance creation — 100th-percentile xA, 96th-percentile shot assists. The quality is real against a competitive comparison pool, not a soft one.
Pre-receive scanning and tempo reading — cognitive habits built before any specific system shaped them. They do not degrade under pressure.
Press resistance and ball retention in tight central areas, already tested in European football before turning 20.
Progressive run engine — 100th percentile. Constant forward availability as a function of directional habit, not sprinting ability.
Set-piece delivery — a deployable weapon from day one at a new club, with no development timeline required.
Composure and international reliability. Nine senior Hungary caps at 20 reflect consistency evidenced by a coaching staff, not projected by a scouting report.
targeted: Aerial duels — 17th percentile. Structural, not developmental. Set-piece defensive planning must account for this before signing, not after.
Dribble success under pressure — 26th percentile. When passing lanes close, the ball can go with them. A coachable gap, but one that surfaces more often in a better league.
Progressive pass frequency — 50th percentile despite 100th-percentile quality. The habit of attempting what he is capable of has to match the quality already present.
System dependency — creation output requires possession context and a protecting pivot. A direct team without that structure will not see the profile this data describes.
verdict.potentialProjection: Projects as a consistent starter and genuine creative influence at a top-five European club. Contributes at Champions League level as a playmaker and chance-creator rather than a controlling midfielder. The ceiling above 8 moves if the progressive pass frequency normalises to match the quality already present.

players.verdictLine: A 22-year-old Dinamo Zagreb midfielder who leads all 1. HNL central midfielders in xA, assists, and combined assist chain simultaneously — ranked 5th among all U23 midfielders in Eastern Europe by the CIES/Impect index. The output is not a function of his teammates. It is despite them.

description: A natural number 10 who moves everywhere in the attacking third, connecting zones rather than occupying one. The playmaking instinct is primary — he prefers the ball to move rather than to carry it, understanding that the pass arrives faster than the run.
When the game opens, he carries at an 89th-percentile rate anyway. Sets pieces are a genuine additional weapon. Works defensively with willingness that his positional profile does not demand.

onBall.title: Moves into pockets and returns to sender — quickly
onBall.text: Comfortable receiving between lines, never looks rushed despite delayed reactions around him. Prefers combination play to carrying, understanding the ball arrives faster than the man. 83rd-percentile progressive passes alongside 89th-percentile progressive runs: he does both, and knows which one the situation calls for.

onBall.title: Final pass is his specialist skill
onBall.text: 100th-percentile xA, 100th-percentile assists, 100th-percentile combined assist chain among all HNL central midfielders. These numbers do not come from volume alone — 88th-percentile xA per shot assist confirms the quality of each individual creative action, not just how many he attempts.

onBall.title: Pulls the trigger with both feet, confidently
onBall.text: 100th-percentile shots per 90. Will shoot from his own half, out of stride, without hesitation. The goal in question — collecting in his own box, running the full length, waiting for the keeper to commit — was a demonstration of how unhurried his decision-making is even at full pace.

onBall.title: First touch under pressure needs attention
onBall.text: Despite his intelligence in finding pockets, the touch that sets up the next action lets him down in tighter situations — even against the slower defensive reactions in the HNL. He tends to play backwards when facing forward play would be available.

onBall.title: Set-piece threat across all delivery types
onBall.text: 93rd-percentile crosses and cross accuracy simultaneously. Dangerous from direct free kicks, deliveries, and offers a rare long-throw option.

offBall.title: Genuinely interested in the defensive phase
offBall.text: 60th-percentile pAdj tackles and interceptions, 62nd-percentile pAdj interceptions — above what the position and the club's style demand. He counter-presses immediately on transition and commits to defensive duels without being instructed to.

offBall.title: Arrives in the box at the right moment
offBall.text: 88th-percentile box touches. Good sense of where space is and where the defensive shape is lax — partly a product of his constant movement, but the timing of arrivals in dangerous areas is a genuine trait rather than lucky positioning.

offBall.title: Reads the game before his teammates
offBall.text: Surrounded by players who see things after they have already happened. The freedom he currently enjoys is partly a product of being significantly smarter than the players around him. At a higher level, that gap closes — and the true ceiling becomes visible for the first time.

offBall.title: Will step into 1v1 duels when not the right choice
offBall.text: Ambitious and adventurous in his defensive reads. Does not always wait for the right moment, occasionally committing when holding position is correct. The competitive instinct is genuine; the spatial discipline in defensive 1v1s needs calibration.

pure.name: Creative Output
pure.desc: 100th-percentile xA, assists, and combined assist chain simultaneously. The vision and execution are real and system-independent.

pure.name: Goal Threat from Midfield
pure.desc: 100th-percentile shots, 98th-percentile npxG. Will shoot from anywhere, both feet, without hesitation.

pure.name: Acceleration and Carrying
pure.desc: 98th-percentile accelerations, 88th-percentile progressive runs. Covers all phases of the attacking third continuously.

context.name: Set-Piece Quality
context.desc: 93rd-percentile crosses and cross accuracy. Dangerous from dead balls — direct free kicks, deliveries, long throws.

context.name: Defensive Willingness
context.desc: Counter-presses immediately, engages in both phases, does not treat the defensive phase as recovery time.

system.name: Teammate Dependency
system.desc: Currently the best player at Dinamo by a measurable gap. The suppression from below-average teammates means statistics represent a floor, not a ceiling.

system.name: Space Creation
system.desc: Output is consistent across settled play, transition, and set pieces — the profile travels across multiple systems.

exposed.name: Temperament
exposed.desc: Three-match suspension for slapping a player. A separate incident involving a kick to an opponent's chest. Requires direct management at the next club, not hope.

exposed.name: First Touch in Tight Pockets
exposed.desc: Forces backwards play in moments where the forward option is available. The gap between what he sees and what he can execute under pressure.

exposed.name: Physical Duel Resistance
exposed.desc: 12th-percentile duels won. Will contest and lose in a higher-intensity central midfield environment.

athletic.label: Stride and acceleration
athletic.value: 98th-percentile accelerations. Long-stride athlete who gets to top speed quickly over 10–15 metres. The carry numbers emerge from this.

athletic.label: Engine
athletic.value: Does not stop running — covers ground in both directions across 90 minutes without visible fatigue.

athletic.label: Physical duels
athletic.value: 12th-percentile duels won. The commitment is not the problem. The physicality for repeated high-intensity contact in a more demanding midfield environment is still developing.

athletic.label: ACL history
athletic.value: Cruciate tear Aug 2023, return Feb 2024. Moving freely, full stride, no visible physical limitation on current tape.

cognitive.label: Game reading
cognitive.value: Sees things before his teammates act on them. The playmaking numbers come from this — consistently identifies the receiver and moment before the defence has recognized the threat.

cognitive.label: Decision speed
cognitive.value: Controlled, never rushed. Smiling in games, unhurried even under pressure. The shot from his own half is the clearest illustration.

cognitive.label: Pass weight
cognitive.value: Sees the option clearly; does not always execute it at the right weight. Touch in tight pockets can force backwards play when forward is available.

cognitive.label: Spatial ambition
cognitive.value: Adventurous in both phases. The same instinct that produces the assist from an unexpected angle also produces the defensive commitment at the wrong moment.

psychological.label: Temperament
psychological.value: The primary non-technical concern. A three-match ban for slapping a player and a separate incident involving a kick to an opponent's chest. The competitive fire is real and is part of what makes him effective.

psychological.label: Ego and environment
psychological.value: Currently the best player at Dinamo by a measurable gap. A player who gets used to operating at a level well below his ceiling can develop habits — of freedom, of dominance — that require adjustment when the context changes.

psychological.label: Composure in big moments
psychological.value: Confident, smiles in games, chirps at referees. He takes responsibility and does not hide from the ball when the game is difficult.

psychological.label: Cards
psychological.value: 27th-percentile cards — moderate, not extreme. The suspension was for a specific incident rather than a chronic card pattern.

devPriorities.title: First touch quality in tight spaces
devPriorities.text: The most important technical task. Seeing the forward option and executing the touch that allows delivery on it are two different skills. The first is already elite. The second occasionally forces the ball backwards even in the current environment.

devPriorities.title: Temperament management in high-stakes moments
devPriorities.text: Not a character concern — a specific competitive impulse that needs a practical outlet. At a club with better teammates and higher-stakes fixtures, the frustration that produces incidents will find more meaningful triggers.

devPriorities.title: Physical robustness in midfield duels
devPriorities.text: 12th-percentile duels won is the profile's most exposed number in a higher-level central midfield environment. Building upper-body strength and shielding technique while retaining the athletic freedom that produces the acceleration numbers.

devPriorities.title: Defensive positioning calibration
devPriorities.text: The pressing instinct is there and is a genuine asset. The moment of commitment — when to step, when to hold — is not yet consistently correct in central 1v1 situations.

prose: The creative output, the movement range, the defensive contribution, and the shooting threat are not products of the 1. HNL. The data would look different with better players around him — almost certainly better, not worse.

The 8.0 is grounded in the profile's completeness. There are no structural technical gaps. The first-touch limitation is a ceiling question, not a floor question. The temperament is the only non-technical concern that warrants genuine attention.
Serie A and Eredivisie are the natural immediate environments — fluid systems that use the number 10 as an orchestrator and give creative players space to connect rather than track.

leagueScores.note: Physical load and transition intensity are the specific challenges. The duel-winning deficit and the first-touch gap both compress under Premier League defensive pressure.
Technical environment rewards exactly the playmaking skillset. Space between lines is more available, tempo is controlled, and possession structures give him the time to execute what he already sees.

Transition speed compresses the creative windows. The shooting instinct and carry in open space suit German football, but the physical intensity in midfield duels arrives faster than his current frame can absorb.

The most natural immediate fit. Compact defensive structures reward spatial intelligence over athleticism. Number 10 roles in Italian football are built around players who orchestrate rather than overpower.
Athletic pace and open game structures suit the carrying and transition instinct. Physical contact in duels is more demanding than Croatia but the creative freedom creates meaningful opportunity.
The other natural immediate fit alongside Serie A. High lines, technical emphasis, and frequent central creative involvement give him the repetition in the role he was built for.
Physically demanding enough to build duel resistance. System variety means fit is club-dependent — at a technically-oriented side with a number 10 role, the output would transfer directly.

riskGauges.label: Development
riskGauges.note: Upward trajectory, 1,639 senior minutes at a high-profile HNL club, ranked 5th among all U23 Eastern European midfielders. First-touch and physical robustness are the development tasks — both are coachable.

riskGauges.label: Psychological
riskGauges.note: Suspension and physical altercation incidents are documented facts. The fire that produces them also produces the defensive pressure and the ambitious carry from his own half. A coach who manages it directly is the key variable.

riskGauges.label: Market
riskGauges.note: €3M for a player leading the league in three assist categories simultaneously, contracted until 2028, at 22. Represented. The valuation has not yet caught up to the output.

riskGauges.label: Systemic
riskGauges.note: The profile travels across multiple systems. He creates in transition, in settled play, from set pieces, and off carries. A slight drop outside ideal conditions but no cliff edge — the skillset is genuinely versatile.

players.trajectoryNote: Projects as a consistent starter and creative fulcrum at a top-division club. The ceiling is higher — a player this complete at 22, being suppressed by his environment, has room to grow into a 9-level contributor given the right setting and coaching.

verdict.statement: A modern number 10 with complete creative output, genuine goal threat, and a defensive contribution that his positional profile does not demand. The data represents a floor, not a ceiling — a player operating well above his current teammates is not being measured at his actual level. The temperament is real, requires direct management, and is the single variable that separates this from a straightforward acquisition case.

Movement range — 98th-percentile accelerations and 88th-percentile progressive runs; covers all phases of the attacking third continuously
Shooting instinct — 100th-percentile shots, both feet, from any distance; the trigger instinct does not require coaching
Set-piece quality — 93rd-percentile crosses and accuracy; additional value that travels unconditionally
Defensive willingness — counter-presses immediately, engages in both phases, does not treat the defensive phase as recovery

First touch in tight pockets — forces backwards play in moments where the forward option is available
Physical duel resistance — 12th-percentile duels won; will contest and lose in a higher-intensity central midfield environment
Defensive positioning precision — steps into 1v1s at the wrong moment; the instinct is good, the spatial discipline is not yet calibrated

The ceiling is higher — a player this complete at 22, being suppressed by his environment, has room to grow into a 9-level contributor given the right setting and coaching. The temperament is the only variable that could prevent the move.

players.verdictLine: A 21-year-old who leads the 1. HNL in box presence and aerial duels among all midfielders — not because he controls games, but because he crashes them. The profile is specific, unusual, and considerably more transferable than it first appears.

description: Covers the full vertical range of the pitch with and without the ball. The attacking orientation is dominant: he positions high, attacks second balls, and arrives into the box with the timing and aggression of a striker rather than a midfielder.
Best deployed as the more advanced of two central midfielders, in a 4-2-3-1 or 4-3-3, where he can operate between the lines and arrive late into the box without leaving a structural gap.

onBall.title: Box arrival timing
onBall.text: His most distinctive offensive habit: he reads crosses and set pieces early and attacks the ball in the air while defenders are still reacting. The majority of his goals this season have been headers, which is unusual for a midfielder and reflects genuine spatial anticipation, not athleticism alone.

onBall.title: Ball carrying over distance
onBall.text: He covers ground with the ball, particularly in transition. His progressive run percentile (98th among HNL midfielders) reflects a habit of driving rather than releasing. He keeps the ball close and changes direction under pressure rather than passing out of it — a Barcelona academy residue that makes him hard to press in isolation.

onBall.title: Two-footed execution
onBall.text: He is right-footed but regularly uses his left for passes and finishes in traffic. The frequency is notable — this is not a desperation foot, it is a functional second option that he deploys selectively.

onBall.title: Short passing accuracy with tempo lapses
onBall.text: His short passing accuracy is around the 16th percentile in the midfield group, but the mechanism matters. When he plays short, he is accurate. The problem is that he sometimes forces tempo and tries to get rid of the ball quickly — the passes that go wrong are the hurried ones rather than the deliberate ones.

onBall.title: First touch inconsistency
onBall.text: When the ball is played sharply into a tight area, his first touch occasionally bounces too far or leaves him side-on. This is not constant — his close control in dribbling situations is genuinely good — but the receive-under-pressure touch is the part of his technical profile that still needs cleaning up.

offBall.title: Roaming movement pattern
offBall.text: He does not stay in one channel. He scans quickly and regularly appears in wide areas, then returns centrally, which confuses man-marking systems and creates positional overloads in the final third. The habit is an asset when the team has the ball; it creates exposure in transition when he is out wide and the team loses possession.

offBall.title: Set-piece positioning
offBall.text: He positions himself specifically for deliveries — near post, back post, the penalty spot — and times his run to arrive late. This is a deliberate movement pattern, not coincidental, and it is what makes his aerial win rate at 100th percentile among HNL midfielders credible despite his frame not being unusually large.

offBall.title: Pressing effort without structure
offBall.text: He presses with energy when the team presses but does not lead the press. His defensive actions against the forward group sit in the 75th percentile, which is real output, but his positioning in recovery is inconsistent. He drops back often but rarely deep.

offBall.title: Transition jogging
offBall.text: A noted tendency to jog rather than sprint in transition, particularly back toward his own half. At Hajduk, who control possession and lead games frequently, this is partially contextual. At a higher level, this will be tested directly and should be monitored.

offBall.title: Tackling quality
offBall.text: When he commits to a tackle, his timing is generally good. His sliding and standing tackle success is solid, and his anticipatory positioning in ground duels — using his frame to shield and redirect — compensates for a strength level that is below average for his size.

pure.name: Box Arrival Intelligence
pure.desc: His ability to read the moment a cross or delivery is played and arrive at the right spot is not pace-dependent. It is a habit of attention — he reads the first phase and positions for the second before most defenders have processed the first. This survives any system or league environment.

pure.name: Two-Footed Execution
pure.desc: Functional use of the left foot in real game situations — not just a fallback. This is set by this age and reflects his Barcelona academy background. Translates directly across environments: defenders cannot shade one side and feel protected.

pure.name: Aerial Heading Ability
pure.desc: He generates power and accuracy from a standing or running jump that outperforms his physical frame. The technical habit — attacking the ball first rather than jumping and hoping — is what produces this. Reliable and transferable.

pure.name: Athletic Genetics
pure.desc: Son of a Lithuanian marathon runner and triple jumper. His natural physical attributes — vertical jump, body composition, recovery capacity — reflect that background. The frame is mesomorphic without being bulky, which should serve him well as he fills out physically.

context.name: Dribbling Effectiveness
context.desc: 89th percentile dribble success against HNL midfielders, 93rd against forwards. Strong in isolation, against a single opponent in space. In tighter, more intense defensive environments this number will come under pressure.

context.name: Shot Creation for Others
context.desc: His xA (76th vs midfielders, 64th vs forwards) suggests he generates half-chances for teammates when he carries into dangerous areas. But this is incidental to his primary mechanism. A system that asks him to be a primary chance creator will find his output drops.

system.name: High Box Presence
system.desc: At Hajduk, who dominate possession and generate cross volume, he arrives in the box frequently. The number — 100th percentile touches in box among HNL midfielders — reflects a system that creates the conditions for him to exploit. The quality remains even as volume compresses.

system.name: Progressive Running Volume
system.desc: 98th percentile progressive runs. In a Hajduk side that transitions quickly and has space to exploit, he drives forward constantly. In a lower block or a team that builds slowly, this trait is compressed. The capacity is there; the environment either activates it or suppresses it.

exposed.name: Pass Selection Under Pressure
exposed.desc: His passing percentile vs midfielders (9th passes per 90, 30th accuracy) is low. The problem is not volume but the rushed passes when he is pressed: he forces it rather than retaining. This is a habit question, not a technical question, which makes it addressable but not automatic.

exposed.name: Defensive Transition Intensity
exposed.desc: The jogging tendency in recovery is partly contextual, partly habitual. At the next level, where pressing intensity is maintained for longer periods, the habit will be exposed in specific match situations.

exposed.name: Injury Resilience (Monitor)
exposed.desc: A cruciate ligament strain in 2023 (70 days, 11 games missed) and an ankle injury in late 2025 (54 days, 4 games missed). Two significant soft-tissue injuries before the age of 22 warrant monitoring.

athletic.label: Frame
athletic.value: 1.81m, mesomorphic build. Slight frame relative to height — not bulky, but not slight. Core strength is sufficient for ground duels currently; physical growth over the next two to three years should close the gap at a higher level.

athletic.label: Pace & Acceleration
athletic.value: Decent, not elite. Covers ground well over distance, turns quickly in tight spaces. His 72nd percentile accelerations are functional rather than explosive.

athletic.label: Aerial Ability
athletic.value: 100th percentile aerial duels per 90 against both midfielders and forwards. Exceptional vertical leap, strong heading technique. The output is not size-dependent — it is timing and aggression.

athletic.label: Strength
athletic.value: Below average for his frame, above average in application. Core positioning in duels compensates for raw strength. Not yet a dominant physical presence in contact.

athletic.label: Injury Context
athletic.value: Two significant injuries before age 22. Cruciate strain (2023), ankle (2025–26). Both recovered within expected timelines. Screening recommended at this valuation.

cognitive.label: Spatial Awareness
cognitive.value: High, particularly in the final third. His box arrival patterns reflect genuine anticipatory reading of the game's next phase. He processes where the ball will land before it leaves the delivery.

cognitive.label: Scanning
cognitive.value: Quick scanner in both phases. He checks his surroundings before receiving, which partly explains his ability to find pocket space. The scanning habit is clearly established — a Barcelona academy marker.

cognitive.label: Decision Speed Under Pressure
cognitive.value: Inconsistent. His best decisions are instinctive — the carry, the box run, the header. When pressed into a passing decision quickly, the tempo occasionally gets ahead of his processing and forces a rushed ball.

cognitive.label: Positional Flexibility
cognitive.value: High tactical adaptability. Can operate as an AM, central 8, or wider midfielder. His versatility is genuine because his scanning and movement patterns do not depend on a fixed starting position.

psychological.label: Competitive Background
psychological.value: Born into elite athletic family. Both parents competed at international level in endurance and power disciplines. The psychological baseline for competition and physical dedication appears embedded rather than coached.

psychological.label: Barcelona Academy Exit
psychological.value: Left Barça, joined Hajduk at 17. Moving from one of the world's most demanding development environments to a first-tier Balkan club required genuine adaptability. The transition has produced consistent senior minutes and tangible output.

psychological.label: Composure in the Box
psychological.value: Strong. His npxG per shot at the 96th percentile among HNL midfielders, 89th among forwards, suggests he is generating and converting high-quality opportunities rather than wasting them.

psychological.label: Communication Style
psychological.value: Quiet on the pitch. Not a vocal organizer. He leads through movement and effort rather than instruction. This is not a concern at 21 — it is a personality type, not a maturity gap.

devPriorities.title: Pass selection in high-pressure moments
devPriorities.text: The technical range is there — he executes short passes accurately and can use both feet. What he does not yet do consistently is slow down to make the right decision when the press arrives. He forces tempo on the receive and the result is occasional turnovers from rushed balls. This is a processing habit, not a technical limitation.

devPriorities.title: Defensive transition intensity
devPriorities.text: The jogging pattern in recovery is observable and documented. At a higher level, the physical cost of consistent sprint-back defending will test this habit directly. He does not currently treat defensive transition as urgently as he treats attacking transition. That needs to change at the next level.

devPriorities.title: First touch quality on sharp, low deliveries
devPriorities.text: His aerial receive is excellent. His ground receive in tight situations still produces errors — the ball occasionally bounces away from his body or leaves him side-on when he needs to be facing forward. The inconsistency in this specific context is visible enough to name.

devPriorities.title: Physical assertiveness in contact
devPriorities.text: His strength application is not yet matching his physical foundation. He wins aerial duels well because his jumping technique is excellent. In ground contact situations, he is functional rather than dominant. This should develop naturally as he fills out physically over the next two to three seasons.

prose: The mechanism of Pukštas's value is unusually portable. His primary mechanism — arriving into dangerous zones, attacking aerial deliveries, and finishing from close range — is not system-dependent in the same way most midfielders' output is. Boxes exist everywhere. Set pieces happen everywhere.

What the 1. HNL inflates is his volume. Hajduk dominate possession and generate cross volume that creates the conditions for his second-phase arrivals to happen frequently. At a higher level that volume will reduce, but the quality-per-opportunity holds: his npxG per shot at 89th percentile against the forward pool is a finishing habit, not a system artifact.

He is ready for most target leagues now, particularly those with verticality and cross-volume in their game model. The risks worth naming are physical intensity and pressing demands — the recovery jogging habit will be tested in leagues where box-to-box midfielders are expected to cover ground at sprint pace in defensive transitions.

leagueScores.note: The box-arrival mechanism translates directly. Best fit: a direct, vertical club rather than a possession-dominant one that asks the eight to build play. Not an automatic starter, but a credible squad option with room to grow.

The strongest single fit. High-intensity pressing environments demand athleticism and vertical drive — both present in this profile. Cross volume and set-piece delivery would give him frequent arrivals. Primary target league.

Good fit, slightly less obvious than Bundesliga. Ligue 1 rewards athletic, box-to-box profiles with goal threat, and his defensive output — functional rather than elite — is acceptable in a league where eights are not always required to be defensive anchors.

More demanding positionally. Serie A midfielders are asked to do more structured defensive work and operate in tighter spaces. An attacking-midfield or trequartista position in a direct Italian side is a better fit than a central-eight role.

La Liga's best clubs ask midfielders to circulate possession and manage tempo — neither is his strength. The lower-half of La Liga, with more direct, counter-attacking teams, is a better fit. System match at elite La Liga level is the weakest of the target leagues.

Near-ideal system fit as a developmental step. High cross volume, attacking-oriented football, physical but not extreme pressing demands. Would give him box arrivals, set-piece opportunities, and competitive minutes.

Comfortable transition. Belgian football is physically direct, rewards athletes, and has the set-piece and cross volume to activate his strongest traits. A Club Brugge or Anderlecht system profile would be very well matched.

riskGauges.label: Development
riskGauges.note: 2,278 senior minutes at 21, consistent output across the full season, already in the top-10 U21 shooting midfielders globally per CIES data. The injury history (two events before age 22) introduces a small flag. The trajectory is clearly upward.

riskGauges.label: Psychological
riskGauges.note: Elite athletic family background, Barcelona academy formation, successful transition to a different culture at 17. He has already managed the psychological complexity of two significant career transitions and come out with increasing output.

riskGauges.label: Market
riskGauges.note: €5M at a club with a 2027 contract. CAA Stellar is a professional and established agency. The CIES ranking, Hajduk's profile, and the US eligibility all create market pressure. The value is fair for this output level.

riskGauges.label: Systemic
riskGauges.note: The core mechanism — box arrivals, aerial threat, progressive carrying — is not system-dependent in the way a pure playmaker's output is. The system risk is real only in possession-dominant, tempo-control environments that ask the eight to be a primary ball-progressor.

players.trajectoryNote: Projects as a consistent contributor at a top-five European club — a player who regularly delivers eight to twelve league goals from midfield, provides aerial threat at set pieces, and drives transitions at pace.

verdict.statement: Pukštas is not a midfielder who scores. He is a scorer who plays in midfield — a distinction that matters both for how you evaluate him and how you deploy him. His box-arrival intelligence, aerial finishing quality, and two-footed execution are formed habits, not projections. The passing volume is low, the defensive positioning is functional rather than excellent, and he occasionally forces tempo rather than managing it. A club that buys him understanding the profile — a direct, goal-contributing eight in a vertical system — gets a player whose primary mechanism has already proved itself against a decent level of competition.
travels: Box arrival timing and second-phase positioning — a habit of attention, not a system outcome
Shot quality selection — 89th percentile npxG per shot against the forward pool; the efficiency is genuine
Aerial heading — 100th percentile against both comparison groups, driven by timing and technique, not frame size
Two-footed execution — a genuine second option from Barcelona academy formation, not a fallback
Dribble success and close control under pressure — 93rd percentile against forwards in isolation situations
Psychological resilience — elite athletic background, two significant injury recoveries, consistent output trajectory
targeted: Rushed passing under pressure — forcing tempo when pressed is the first habit top opponents will exploit
Defensive transition intensity — the recovery jogging pattern will be tested in every environment above this one
First touch on sharp ground deliveries — inconsistent enough at this level to become a genuine problem at the next
Physical assertiveness in contact — raw force in collisions does not yet match frame and genetic baseline
Injury monitoring — two soft-tissue events before age 22; full medical screening required at €5M
verdict.potentialProjection: An 8 projects as a consistent contributor at a top-five European club — a player who regularly delivers eight to twelve league goals from midfield, provides aerial threat at set pieces that opponents do not expect from a midfielder, and drives transitions at pace. The ceiling depends less on further development than on system fit.

players.verdictLine: Plays with the composure and tactical intelligence of someone three or four years older. The playmaking output is genuine and already elite for his league.

A 19-year-old inside forward who ranked 2nd among all U21 wingers in Europe's non-big-five leagues on the CIES/Impect index. The explosiveness is genuine, the creation numbers are strong, and the left-foot dependency is the thing to watch.

description: Receives wide on the right and drives inward onto his left foot, looking for the shot lane rather than the crossing position. His value is in the first two seconds after receiving: the first step separates him from his marker before defenders can set, and what follows is pressure, chaos, and usually a left-footed attempt from wherever he ends up.

Best behind a striker with width provided by the fullback. The 7th-ranked player in accelerations per 90 among all U21 offensive players in top divisions by SkillCorner tells you the engine is real before you see a single touch.

onBall.title: Attacks his marker immediately upon receiving.
onBall.text: Does not check the ball or reset. The decision to drive at the defender is made before contact, which means the first step is already in motion by the time the opponent reacts. This is what the acceleration numbers measure; they describe a choice, not just a physical attribute.

onBall.title: Keeps the ball tight under contact from multiple defenders.
onBall.text: Body posture looks precarious while dribbling — a slight forward lean that makes him look like he might lose the ball — but he almost never does. The balance is functional, not aesthetic. Balkan defenders who want to kill the play find him surprisingly difficult to dispossess.

onBall.title: Cuts inside onto his left and shoots with conviction from any angle.
onBall.text: The 73rd-percentile shots volume and shoot-first mentality mean defenders cannot settle into passive positions. He will pull the trigger from positions where most wingers would recycle. Whether it goes in is secondary to whether the habit forces defensive reactions.

onBall.title: Creates for teammates off the dribble with genuine quality.
onBall.text: 87th-percentile shot assists and 73rd-percentile xA identify him as a winger who creates for others while also pursuing his own shot. Smart passes at the 81st percentile suggest the vision is there even when he chooses not to use it.

onBall.title: Overwhelmingly left-footed in execution.
onBall.text: Almost every shot, every significant dribble, every cross attempt involves the left foot. The right foot exists for shielding and balance. At higher levels, where defenders have more time to set and force the weaker foot, it will be targeted.

offBall.title: Explodes forward immediately on ball recovery.
offBall.text: One of the first players to attack space in transition. Does not wait to confirm possession is secure — reads the moment the press is won and commits immediately to the attacking run.

offBall.title: Positions wide and early to create 1v1 opportunities.
offBall.text: Drops to receive away from defenders in wide channels, looking to isolate rather than combine. The move is about manufacturing the duel, not about linking play.

offBall.title: Tracks back and presses with genuine intensity.
offBall.text: 76th-percentile successful defensive actions and 64th-percentile pAdj tackles and interceptions are meaningful for an inside forward. The defensive contribution reflects willingness and tempo, not repositioning reluctantly. A right midfielder role is viable if the occasion demands it.

offBall.title: Uses momentum rather than physical wrestling.
offBall.text: Avoids prolonged contact situations and does not try to hold ground against defenders who outweigh him. Looks to attack space before defenders can set their feet. The 1.80m frame is adequate; the light-contact preference is a deliberate choice rather than a physical limitation.

pure.name: First-step acceleration
pure.desc: 7th among all U21 offensive players in top divisions by accelerations per 90. The separation is immediate — the threat is created before defenders can respond.

pure.name: Ball retention under contact
pure.desc: The unconventional forward lean conceals genuine body control. Balkan senior defenders find him difficult to dispossess. This travels.

pure.name: Creation output
pure.desc: 87th-percentile shot assists, 88th-percentile cross accuracy, 81st-percentile smart passes. The vision and delivery exist alongside the shoot-first profile.

context.name: Pressing intensity
context.desc: 76th-percentile defensive actions is real, but the opposition quality in the SuperLiga is a factor. The willingness is genuine; the difficulty level will increase.

system.name: Defensive resilience without the ball
system.desc: Best in transition-heavy systems that create wide isolations. In settled possession structures his influence drops and the decision inconsistency is exposed in tighter spaces.

exposed.name: Right foot
exposed.desc: Almost every significant action uses the left foot. Defenders at higher levels will steer him right and wait. The development window is now.

exposed.name: Shot quality
exposed.desc: 13th-percentile npxG per shot. Volume compensates currently; at the next level, where closing speed is faster, it will not fully compensate.

athletic.label: Acceleration
athletic.value: The headline attribute. 7th among all U21 offensive players in top divisions by accelerations per 90 per SkillCorner. The separation in the first two steps is what makes him difficult to plan against.

athletic.label: Endurance
athletic.value: Strong engine. Maintains pressing intensity and attacking runs across 90 minutes. Nearly 2,000 minutes at Radnik without an injury history suggests the physical base is robust.

athletic.label: Agility
athletic.value: Sharp directional changes with good balance. The odd dribbling posture conceals genuine body control — the lean that looks like he might fall is the same lean that keeps defenders guessing.

athletic.label: Strength
athletic.value: Light frame. Less effective when defenders get tight and hold him off with body weight. At higher levels, more defenders will make that contact deliberately.

cognitive.label: Decision speed
cognitive.value: Fast when instinctive, inconsistent when multiple options are present. When the decision is drive-and-shoot, execution is immediate. When the game asks him to choose between dribble, pass, and shoot simultaneously, the hesitation is visible.

cognitive.label: Risk profile
cognitive.value: Aggressive and consistent. Prefers action over control, takes responsibility, and seeks the ball rather than hiding after errors. High-variance: produces danger repeatedly, but also cedes possession in ways more conservative players would not.

cognitive.label: Spatial awareness
cognitive.value: Good in transition and in wide isolation. Reads 1v1 positions intelligently. Weaker in settled positional phases where movement is less intuitive.

cognitive.label: Decision hierarchy
cognitive.value: Similar situations can produce dribble, shot, or pass without a visible pattern. The vision is there — 81st-percentile smart passes — but the trigger for using it is not yet reliable.

psychological.label: Mentality
psychological.value: Fighter. Demands the ball, embraces duels, presses willingly. No evidence of avoiding involvement after errors. The shoot-first profile is a psychological as much as technical feature.

psychological.label: Consistency
psychological.value: Impact arrives in bursts rather than as sustained control. A 7/30 shooting run does not appear to alter either volume or approach. The resilience to sustain a high-attempt model despite variable returns is a real attribute.

psychological.label: Big game performance
psychological.value: Stable confidence across match contexts. Composure under defensive pressure from multiple defenders simultaneously is a genuine psychological strength.

psychological.label: Durability
psychological.value: No injury history. ~1,928 minutes at Radnik. Physical and psychological availability across a full season, at 19, with this intensity of movement and contact volume, is worth noting.

devPriorities.title: Build a clearer decision hierarchy in the final third.
devPriorities.text: The raw materials for all three outcomes — dribble, shoot, pass — are present. The 87th-percentile shot assists confirm he can create for teammates; the 81st-percentile smart passes confirm the vision exists. What is inconsistent is the selection process when all three options are available. Reducing volatility in identical situations will lift the end-product reliability significantly without requiring any new skills.

devPriorities.title: Develop the right foot as at least a viable threat.
devPriorities.text: The left-foot dependency is currently manageable because he generates directional variety spatially — going both ways while finishing left. At higher levels, where defensive shape is better organized and defenders have more time to steer him right, the absence of a right-foot threat becomes a structural problem. The development window is now, not later.

devPriorities.title: Improve physical robustness in contact situations.
devPriorities.text: The frame at 1.80m is there. The habit of avoiding prolonged contact is sensible now but will limit effectiveness when defenders in better leagues routinely get tight and hold ground. Shielding technique and the ability to draw fouls from physical positioning — rather than from momentum alone — is the next layer of the profile.

prose: The acceleration, the dribbling under pressure, the defensive willingness, and the creation output are not products of the SuperLiga environment. They are observable in how he plays regardless of the opposition quality. Those travel. A club acquiring Owusu knows what it is getting in the first two seconds of any attacking sequence.

The score sits at 7.5 because the left-foot dependency and the decision inconsistency are genuine questions at the next level, not minor concerns. They are coachable — the ranking among U21 wingers across Europe confirms the physical and creative base is real — but coachable is not the same as already resolved. The right system speeds that resolution considerably.

The best immediate environment is one that creates space through vertical play, tolerates high-attempt profiles, and does not ask him to hold width patiently. Eredivisie and Jupiler Pro League are the structural fits. La Liga's technical environment would accelerate the creative development but expose the decision inconsistency in tighter spaces before it is ready to handle them.

leagueScores.note: Explosiveness and directness translate but decision volatility and physical fragility in contact situations would be punished before the other qualities could establish themselves. Not the right first move out of the SuperLiga.

The technical environment suits the dribbling quality. The slower rhythm and compactness of defensive blocks expose decision inconsistency and the left-foot dependency more than a transition-heavy league would.
Transition-heavy play, wide isolations, and vertical attacks amplify the acceleration and 1v1 threat. The best top-five environment for his profile right now, though not the optimal first step.
Tactical structure limits freedom to attack instinctively. Decision hesitation is punished in tight spaces. The profile does not fit Serie A's typical demands for wingers in positional structures.

Athletic pace and isolation opportunities suit the profile. The open nature of Ligue 1 defensive structures gives him room to generate momentum before defenders can get set. Impact likely streaky, but present.

Open games, high transitions, and frequent 1v1 opportunities maximize explosiveness and confidence. The natural first step out of the SuperLiga for this profile — and likely where the decision hierarchy develops fastest.

Physical demands test the frame without overwhelming it. Belgian clubs regularly deploy direct wide players in exactly the transition-first structures that suit him. A legitimate bridge alongside the Eredivisie.

riskGauges.label: Development
riskGauges.note: No injury history, nearly 2,000 senior minutes at 19, ranked 2nd among U21 wingers in Europe's non-big-five leagues. The left-foot dependency and decision consistency are the open questions — neither is a ceiling, both are coachable with the right environment.

riskGauges.label: Psychological
riskGauges.note: Resilient after errors, demands the ball in difficult moments, presses willingly. The shoot-first mentality that continues regardless of recent returns signals a psychological profile that is not fragile.

riskGauges.label: Market
riskGauges.note: €700K for the 2nd-ranked U21 winger in non-big-five Europe is not a valuation that reflects the output. The market has not yet processed what the CIES ranking, the SkillCorner acceleration data, and the top-10 U20 take-on chart are collectively saying.

riskGauges.label: Systemic
riskGauges.note: A clearly defined best environment: vertical, transition-heavy, wide isolations, tolerance for high-attempt profiles. In settled possession systems or compact defensive structures, the impact drops significantly.

players.trajectoryNote: A 7 projects as a regular starter at top-division level in a transition-based league, with consistent attacking threat through pace and 1v1 ability. If the decision hierarchy and right-foot threat develop, the number moves.

verdict.statement: A high-variance, high-threat inside forward whose game is built on explosiveness, 1v1 dominance, and a shoot-first mentality. He consistently creates danger through individual action, running past defenders as though they do not exist and keeping the ball under pressure that would force most wide players to release early. The ceiling is defined by two things: whether the decision hierarchy in the final third stabilises, and whether a right-foot threat ever develops. Both are open questions. The floor, even without those developments, is a winger who contributes at a level that justifies significantly more than the current valuation.
travels: Elite acceleration and 1v1 separation — top-10 U20 take-ons globally, 7th in U21 accelerations per 90 across top divisions
Ball retention under contact from multiple defenders — tight body control despite unconventional posture
Creation output — 87th-percentile shot assists, 88th-percentile cross accuracy, 81st-percentile smart passes
Defensive work rate — 76th-percentile defensive actions, viable in right midfield role
