players.verdictLine: He is 18 years old, playing senior football in a team on the periphery of Europe, and the data already looks like that of a player three years older. The interceptions are elite. The aerial numbers are not. Those two facts sit at the centre of everything.

description: A ball-playing centre-back with genuine defensive intelligence at 18. Kumar's best position is right centre-back in a back four, where his right foot is dominant and his reading of the game lets him defend proactively rather than reactively.
He has spent significant time as left CB in a back three and in midfield roles at youth level, producing unusually complete spatial awareness for a player at his stage.
Comfortable in possession, capable with both feet, and already showing the positional habits that distinguish defenders who make it at the next level.

onBall.title: Receives cleanly under pressure.
onBall.text: Opens his hips before the ball arrives and plays forward quickly. His 87th-percentile pass accuracy reflects a player who is rarely in trouble on the receive. He protects the ball without hiding from it.

onBall.title: Steps into midfield when space opens.
onBall.text: One of the clearer markers of his youth-team midfield background. He recognises the moment to carry and acts on it, rather than waiting for permission. The progression is deliberate rather than speculative.

onBall.title: Accurate long passer at the 64th percentile among HNL CBs.
onBall.text: He can switch play and hit the far side under pressure. Not yet the elite diagonal range the position demands at the highest level, but already better than average in a decent domestic context.

onBall.title: Comfortable on his left foot.
onBall.text: Predominantly right-footed but does not refuse the ball to his weaker side. Plays the short option left without hesitation; the longer ball from the left is where quality drops.

onBall.title: Third assist involvement in the top 15% of HNL defenders.
onBall.text: Contributing to attacking phases as a player who understands how sequences begin. For an 18-year-old CB, that is unusual.

offBall.title: Intercepts before the situation develops.
offBall.text: The 99th-percentile pAdj interceptions figure is the most important number in this report. He reads the game ahead of the ball rather than reacting to it. A cognitive habit that shows up consistently and early.

offBall.title: Cuts passing lanes by positioning, not by lunging.
offBall.text: Shot blocks at the 98th percentile, pAdj sliding tackles at the 13th. He is everywhere the ball needs to not go. The rarity of sliding challenges is the tell — this is positional defending, not athletic defending.

offBall.title: Disciplined and rarely penalised.
offBall.text: Fouls at the 90th percentile for cleanliness. He almost never gives away free kicks. A player who defends under control rather than gambling for the block.

offBall.title: Aerial win rate sits in the bottom third.
offBall.text: At 27th percentile in aerial duel win rate among HNL CBs, there is a gap between his physical profile and what the numbers say he produces in the air. He wins volume (53rd percentile), but the rate is low.

offBall.title: Organises quietly.
offBall.text: Not yet a formal leader at senior level, but already showing the positioning cues and body language of someone who understands the defensive line as a unit.

pure.name: Positional anticipation
pure.desc: The 99th-percentile interception figure is not a product of Istra's system. It reflects a cognitive habit that operates regardless of team structure. He positions himself to cut passing lanes before they open. This is a habit of attention, and those travel unconditionally.

pure.name: Composure and press resistance in possession
pure.desc: An 87th-percentile pass accuracy at 18, in a system that asks him to build, confirms that his composure under pressure is already formed as a technical habit. This will not wilt when the press is faster or the space tighter.

pure.name: Disciplined defensive approach
pure.desc: Rarely slides, rarely fouls, rarely gets booked. These are not symptoms of passivity. They are symptoms of a player who wins the encounter before the tackle is required. A 90th-percentile cleanliness rate is a meaningful signal that will transfer.

context.name: Ball-carrying into midfield
context.desc: His willingness to step out from the defensive line and carry into space is a strength in the HNL, where the press is less coordinated. In leagues where pressing traps are more structured, the same habit becomes a risk if the timing is slightly off.

system.name: Long-range distribution quality
system.desc: Sits at 64th percentile in long pass accuracy among HNL CBs. Above average for this context, but well below the baseline demand of a top-five league where switching play diagonally under a press is a recurring structural requirement.

exposed.name: Aerial duel win rate
exposed.desc: 27th percentile among HNL CBs at 190cm is the figure that will generate the most scrutiny. He wins enough duels by volume (53rd percentile) but the rate suggests opponents are winning more aerial contests than his frame should allow. An application problem, not a structural one — but it requires deliberate work.

exposed.name: Duel win rate in open play
exposed.desc: 37th percentile in overall duels won reflects a player who is not yet physically dominant in contested situations. At 18 and still developing physically, this is not a concern at this level. It will become one in leagues where physical authority is a baseline requirement.

athletic.label: Frame
athletic.value: 190cm, still filling out. Tall but not yet imposing — the physical foundation is there to grow into a genuinely dominant defender with deliberate strength work over the next two or three years.

athletic.label: Pace
athletic.value: Decent top speed for his size. Not elite over the first step. Compensates with positioning: he rarely needs to sprint because he is rarely caught wrong.

athletic.label: Strength
athletic.value: Not yet physically imposing. Wins duels by timing and anticipation more than by body strength. The duel win rate reflects this. Will change as he matures physically.

athletic.label: Agility
athletic.value: Good agility for his height. Changes direction cleanly. Footwork in tight situations is tidy, and the midfield background is visible in how he moves in confined space.

athletic.label: Aerial
athletic.value: 190cm should produce a higher aerial win rate than 27th percentile suggests. The physical tool exists. The timing and assertiveness in aerial challenges is an application question, not a structural one.

cognitive.label: Attention
cognitive.value: Elite level for his age. Reads the game before the ball arrives. Tracks runners early, reads passing lanes, and positions for the second phase. The interception numbers confirm this is a consistent habit.

cognitive.label: Decision Speed
cognitive.value: Quick in possession. Takes an extra beat on the longer ball under pressure. This is where higher-level opponents in pressing systems will find the seam.

cognitive.label: Risk Profile
cognitive.value: Controlled risk appetite. Attempts progressive passes and carries without forcing them. Almost never commits recklessly. The discipline is a genuine character trait.

cognitive.label: Adaptability
cognitive.value: Has played RCB, LCB, and midfield roles. Absorbs positional instruction. The tactical flexibility is already demonstrated, not a projection.

psychological.label: Maturity
psychological.value: Exceptional for his age. His U17 World Cup performances for Croatia came with composure that typically takes years of senior exposure to produce. He did not look overawed.

psychological.label: Composure
psychological.value: A clean disciplinary record in senior football at 18 says something specific: he does not panic under pressure and convert that panic into a foul or a rash challenge.

psychological.label: Professionalism
psychological.value: Went through the full Istra academy, earned his senior debut in context: the World Cup performance pushed him in, and he held the place.

psychological.label: Leadership
psychological.value: Not yet a formal leader at senior level, but the organisational habits are already visible in positioning cues and body language.

devPriorities.title: Aerial duel win rate
devPriorities.text: This is the clearest gap in an otherwise advanced profile. 27th percentile at 190cm is not a size problem. It is an assertiveness and timing problem. Work on aerial aggression and jump timing will address this more directly than physical conditioning alone. Unresolved, it becomes a reliable route for opponents at the next level.

devPriorities.title: Physical authority in duels
devPriorities.text: 37th percentile in overall duels won is a function of age and physical stage, not technique. The strength work required over the next two to three years is not optional if he wants to compete in top-five leagues. The frame is there. The gym programme needs to be deliberate and progressive.

devPriorities.title: Long-range distribution under pressing conditions
devPriorities.text: The 64th percentile in long pass accuracy is already better than average for this league. The next-level demand is switching play diagonally with weight and precision when the press is coordinated and the window is narrower.

devPriorities.title: Timing on the carry into midfield
devPriorities.text: The instinct to step out of the defensive line is a strength. In more sophisticated pressing environments, that same habit becomes a risk if the timing is half a second off. He needs accumulated exposure to high-press systems to recalibrate.

prose: The cognitive and technical base is already at a level that transfers. Positional anticipation, composure under pressure, and a clean disciplined approach to defending are all habits, not products of a specific system.

The 6.5 reflects two honest constraints. First, the physical gaps: the aerial win rate and duel strength are genuine limitations at this moment, and they will be tested immediately in any top-ten league. Second, the age: 18 is early to project transfer readiness with confidence.

The right next environment is one that tolerates physical development while demanding defensive intelligence. A mid-table Bundesliga, Eredivisie, or Serie A club with a clear pathway would extract the most from him over the next two seasons.

leagueScores.note: Too early. The physical demands of the Premier League require a level of duel authority he does not yet have. The cognitive profile is already close; the body is not.
A realistic medium-term destination. The pressing intensity would test his carry timing but reward his anticipation. A loan at a lower Bundesliga club first would be the sensible path.
Similar physical demands to the Bundesliga with slightly less pressing complexity. The aerial gap would be exposed at set pieces.
The tactical and positional demands of Serie A suit his profile well. Less reliance on brute physicality than the Premier League or Bundesliga. A mid-table club would be a reasonable immediate step.
Spanish defensive structures are positional and systematic, which plays directly to his strengths. The question is physical authority against La Liga strikers.

The most realistic immediate next step. Less physically brutal than the top five leagues, high on possession and positional quality. One strong season here would sharpen the transfer case considerably.
An even cleaner fit at this stage. Physically manageable, tactically demanding, and a league with a track record of developing young CBs for the next step.

riskGauges.label: Development
riskGauges.note: The physical gaps — aerial win rate, duel authority — are real and will be tested immediately at the next level. The frame suggests the upside is genuine, but physical development at 18 is not linear.

riskGauges.label: Psychological
riskGauges.note: U17 World Cup composure, clean disciplinary record, full academy pathway completed without incident. The maturity is already visible in how he handles senior football at 18.

riskGauges.label: Market
riskGauges.note: €350k valuation with contract to June 2030. No competitive bidding pressure, no short runway. The market has not yet priced the profile correctly — acquisition cost is low relative to ceiling.

riskGauges.label: Systemic
riskGauges.note: The profile is readable across most defensive systems. The one constraint: a system that requires immediate physical dominance in aerial duels will expose the current gap before development catches up.

players.trajectoryNote: A starting-level centre-back at a top-five league club within four to five years, if the physical development follows the cognitive trajectory. The ceiling is genuine. The Eredivisie or Jupiler Pro League is the right next environment to test it.

verdict.statement: A centre-back whose defensive intelligence is already elite and whose physical development is still two to three years from its ceiling. Buy him now for what the cognitive profile already is. Do not buy him expecting physical dominance before he is ready for it. The Jupiler Pro League or Eredivisie extracts the most from this profile over the next two seasons.
travels: Elite positional anticipation — 99th-percentile pAdj interceptions is a cognitive habit, not a system product
Composure under pressure and clean pass accuracy already set as technical habits at 18
Exceptional disciplinary record — defends under control, almost never commits a foul
Tactical flexibility across back four and back three roles; midfield background visible in movement
Psychological maturity well ahead of his age, demonstrated under World Cup pressure
targeted: Aerial duel win rate at 27th percentile — will be targeted at set pieces immediately
Overall duel win rate at 37th percentile — physical authority in contested situations is not yet there
Long-range distribution quality needs to step up for top-five league demands
Carry timing into midfield requires recalibration against more structured pressing systems
verdict.potentialProjection: A starting-level centre-back at a top-five league club within four to five years, if the physical development follows the cognitive trajectory. The ceiling is genuine. The Eredivisie or Jupiler Pro League is the right next environment to test it. This is not a player to leave in the HNL for another two seasons while he waits.

players.verdictLine: Most nineteen-year-old wingers at mid-table clubs produce one thing worth watching. Pnevmonidis produces shot assists, xA, progressive passes, smart passes, carry volume, and fouls drawn at elite rates simultaneously. The final output has not caught up yet, but the process is already telling you something the scoreline isn't.

description: A right-footed winger deployed on the left who creates through the cut inside rather than the cross. Low centre of gravity, compact and powerful for his age, with the close control and dribble success to manufacture space at the edge of the box.
He does not stay wide. He drifts inside, overloads central channels, and delivers: key passes, progressive sequences, occasionally the finish from a cut-back.
The playmaking bag is already elite for his age and context. The question the data raises is whether he becomes a scorer as well as a creator at the next level.

onBall.title: Creates through the cut inside, not the byline cross.
onBall.text: Right-footed on the left, he uses width to invite the defender and then drives inward. The 95th-percentile dribble success rate is built on body feints and low-centre changes of direction, not pace alone.

onBall.title: Progressive passing at the 98th percentile.
onBall.text: He does not just carry to the box and shoot into a body. He carries to threatening positions and then delivers. The xA at 92nd percentile confirms the delivery is converting too.

onBall.title: Shot assist volume at 93rd percentile.
onBall.text: Creates finishing opportunities for others at an elite rate. The touches in the box are low (21st percentile), which suggests some reluctance to finish himself.

onBall.title: Key passes at 92nd percentile.
onBall.text: Calm decision-making once in threatening positions. He sees the right pass before he needs to and delivers it without a wasted touch.

onBall.title: Carries into the final third at the 86th percentile.
onBall.text: Ball-carrying to the danger zone is a consistent mechanism, not an occasional flash. Combined with the progressive pass rate, he is regularly arriving at the edge of the box with the ball.

offBall.title: Defends with genuine intensity.
offBall.text: 80th-percentile pAdj tackles and interceptions, 78th-percentile defensive actions, 95th-percentile sliding tackles. For a winger, this is not cosmetic pressing — he closes effectively and wins the ball in advanced areas.

offBall.title: Fouls drawn at 85th percentile.
offBall.text: Gets fouled regularly, confirming opponents cannot stop him cleanly. Combined with the 93rd-percentile duels won, he is genuinely difficult to contain in 1v1 situations.

offBall.title: Presses intelligently, not just energetically.
offBall.text: The pressing angles are disciplined. He presses in ways that cut off escape routes rather than chasing the man — a cognitive quality visible in the interception numbers rather than just the tackle count.

offBall.title: Shot volume is low at 27th percentile.
offBall.text: Creates shots for others at an elite rate but does not manufacture them for himself at the same frequency. Whether it reflects a role instruction or a genuine tendency to defer at the last moment is not fully resolved.

offBall.title: Moves intelligently when Atromitos plays out of possession.
offBall.text: Tracks his fullback's runs, creates space for the switch of play, and shifts roles fluidly. The positional reading is already well ahead of his age.

pure.name: 1v1 dribble quality
pure.desc: The 95th-percentile dribble success is not a product of weak Greek Super League full-backs. The mechanism is technical: low centre of gravity, sharp body feints, close control that keeps the ball available through contact. Those are physical and technical habits that travel.

pure.name: Calm decision-making under pressure
pure.desc: Multiple independent observations confirm the same quality: he processes and delivers without panic in tight moments. Visible in the key pass rate and the xA — a cognitive habit rather than a context product.

pure.name: Defensive work rate and intensity
pure.desc: The pressing numbers are too high and too consistent to be explained by tactical instruction alone. He presses because he wants to press. That unconditional defensive engagement travels regardless of system or manager preference.

context.name: Role flexibility across phases
context.desc: Has operated as a left winger, attacking midfielder, and second striker in different Atromitos phases. The adaptability is already demonstrated. A system that requires one rigid role gets less out of this profile than one that gives him positional freedom inside the final third.

system.name: Progressive passing volume
system.desc: 98th percentile among Greek Super League wingers. The vision and execution are real. Whether the volume holds at the next level depends on how much time and space he receives. The quality travels; the volume is environment-dependent.

exposed.name: Shot creation for self
exposed.desc: 27th-percentile shot volume and 21st-percentile touches in the opposition box is the clearest gap. He creates excellent opportunities for teammates but does not yet arrive inside the box often enough or pull the trigger when he does. Wingers who only create for others are easier to account for defensively.

exposed.name: Cross accuracy
exposed.desc: 20th percentile. He cuts inside by default and rarely crosses, but when he does, the delivery is below average. This limits the threat he poses when opponents crowd the central channel and force him wide.

athletic.label: Frame
athletic.value: Approximately 1.78m with a compact, powerful build. Low centre of gravity is the key physical attribute — it gives him balance through contact and enables the rapid direction changes that define how he beats defenders.

athletic.label: Speed
athletic.value: Explosively quick over short distances. Accelerates into space convincingly and consistently troubles defenders in transition.

athletic.label: Agility
athletic.value: The standout physical quality. Changes direction cleanly and quickly, maintains close control through contact, and uses body feints to manufacture separation in tight spaces.

athletic.label: Aerial
athletic.value: 82nd percentile in aerial duel win rate, unusually high for a winger of his frame. Context: sample size is small. Worth noting but should not be weighted heavily.

athletic.label: Stamina
athletic.value: Presses effectively for the full ninety minutes and contributes defensively late in games. The intensity does not drop.

cognitive.label: Decision speed
cognitive.value: Fast and calm. Processes passing options before the carry ends and delivers without hesitation once in position. The key pass and xA numbers are partly a reflection of this.

cognitive.label: Spatial reading
cognitive.value: Understands the geometry of the attacking third intuitively. Drifts inside when the space opens, holds wide when the fullback needs the overlap, and reads the switch of play before it arrives.

cognitive.label: Pressing intelligence
cognitive.value: Presses in angles that cut escape routes, not just angles that reach the ball fastest. The interception numbers alongside the tackle numbers confirm this distinction.

cognitive.label: Risk calibration
cognitive.value: The 95th-percentile dribble success is partly because he is selective. He does not attempt the 1v1 in the wrong moment. When to carry and when to play is already a calibrated decision.

psychological.label: Resilience
psychological.value: Raised primarily by his mother after losing his father at a young age. That background shapes a player's relationship with adversity in ways that do not show up in a spreadsheet.

psychological.label: Composure
psychological.value: Calm under pressure in the most literal sense: he does not rush decisions when defenders are close and the action is consequential. That calm is visible consistently, not occasionally.

psychological.label: Engagement
psychological.value: A winger who presses at the 80th percentile for pAdj tackles and interceptions is a winger who cares about the team's defensive phase, not just his own attacking moments. That quality makes him coachable.

psychological.label: Ambition
psychological.value: Olympiacos academy product representing Greece at U21 level at 19 while on loan in the top domestic division. The pathway is accelerating.

devPriorities.title: Shot volume and box presence
devPriorities.text: The creation metrics are elite. The finishing output is not. He needs to arrive inside the penalty area more often and pull the trigger when he does. A winger who creates only for others is a manageable defensive problem at the next level.

devPriorities.title: Cross delivery quality
devPriorities.text: 20th percentile when he does cross. When opponents crowd the central channel and force him wide, the cross becomes his only option. Right now it is below average. This is a technical repetition problem: weight, timing, and selection of the crossing moment.

devPriorities.title: Maintaining progressive passing volume in higher-tempo environments
devPriorities.text: The 98th-percentile progressive passes are real, but some of the volume is produced in moments of relative comfort that higher-press environments will remove. The quality will hold; the volume will compress.

prose: The creation and dribbling qualities are already functioning at a level that transfers. The composure, the pressing, the progressive vision: these are formed habits that will show up in a better environment.

The 6.0 reflects two specific constraints. First, the sample: 638 minutes across a loan season at a mid-table club is not enough to draw firm conclusions. Second, the goal threat gap: a winger who does not yet create shots for himself regularly is a partial profile.

The right next environment is one that gives him positional freedom inside the final third rather than demanding he stay wide. He is not a touchline runner who crosses — he is a half-space carrier who plays through the lines.

leagueScores.note: Too early at any club. The Premier League's tempo and defensive organisation compresses exactly the spaces he currently exploits.
A realistic medium-term destination rather than an immediate one. The pressing model suits his defensive work rate. The shot volume gap would be exposed immediately.

Ligue 1's physical and transition-heavy nature is a decent fit for his athleticism and pressing aggression. The creation metrics would remain above average; the goal threat question would remain unanswered.
Serie A's tactical sophistication suits his spatial intelligence. But Italian defensive organisation is specifically designed to neutralise half-space carriers.
A possession-based La Liga side would give him the most build-up time. The 1v1 quality travels well in Spanish football. The cross accuracy gap is the main exposure.
The most realistic immediate next step. Dutch football rewards exactly the profile he offers: progressive carrying, creative passing, pressing intelligence, and technical 1v1 quality.
The best-fit immediate environment. Physically manageable, tactically demanding, and a league that has developed several wide forwards with similar profiles into higher-level transfers.

riskGauges.label: Development
riskGauges.note: The core creation and dribbling qualities are already formed. Development risk centres on the goal threat gap, which is a real but coachable problem at 19.

riskGauges.label: Psychological
riskGauges.note: Composed under pressure, engaged defensively, no signs of entitlement or disengagement. Personal background suggests an above-average relationship with adversity.

riskGauges.label: Market
riskGauges.note: €1M valuation with Olympiacos parent contract to 2028. No competitive acquisition pressure. The market has not priced the profile fully.

riskGauges.label: Systemic
riskGauges.note: Profile works across multiple systems provided he gets positional freedom in the final third. The one constraint: a team that requires the winger to stay wide and deliver crosses will expose the cross accuracy gap.

players.trajectoryNote: A first-choice wide forward at a Europa League-level club within three to four years if the goal threat develops alongside the creation quality already in place. The ceiling moves toward 8.5 if the finishing resolves.

verdict.statement: Buy the creation quality, the dribbling technique, and the defensive engagement. Develop the finishing instinct and the cross delivery. The Jupiler Pro League is the right next environment: physically manageable, tactically demanding, and a context where the goal numbers will be tested seriously for the first time.
travels: Dribble success at 95th percentile: a trained technique, not an athletic product; scales with level
Progressive passing vision: 98th percentile with elite xA confirms the delivery matches the intent
Defensive pressing quality: unconditional and intelligent; presses angles, not just the man
Composure under pressure: formed habit, not a context product; visible consistently across the season
Fouls drawn at 85th percentile: opponents cannot stop him cleanly; that does not change with the level
targeted: Shot volume at 27th percentile: creates elite chances for others, does not yet pull the trigger enough himself
Box presence at 21st percentile: needs to arrive inside the penalty area more often and with more intent
Cross accuracy at 20th percentile: when opponents crowd the half-space, the wide option becomes the only one
verdict.potentialProjection: A first-choice wide forward at a Europa League-level club within three to four years if the goal threat develops alongside the creation quality already in place. The ceiling moves toward 8.5 if the finishing resolves. The floor, given the creation and pressing base, is a consistently useful wide player at that level. At €1M, either outcome represents strong value.

players.verdictLine: There is a version of this report where you read the numbers, note they are impressive for a 20-year-old, and move on. The more interesting version is the one where you stop and ask how many midfielders his age are already combining these things simultaneously: carrying through pressure, switching play constantly, sliding across transitions cleanly, receiving responsibility under pressure, and doing all of it without the profile feeling chaotic. Trabzonspor paid €5.5M for what Oulaï was becoming. Someone else is going to pay considerably more for the version already starting to appear now.

description: A genuine 6–8 hybrid who is better as a 6 right now, though not by much. Receives from the centre-backs with composure, drives forward through pressure, and plays the line-breaking pass when the moment arrives rather than when it becomes safe.

The final-third production is still developing. The carrying and passing range are not. One of Trabzonspor's most deployed midfielders this season at 20 years old, which tells you something about the level of trust the club already has in the profile.

onBall.title: Receives from the centre-backs without turning away from pressure
onBall.text: 95th-percentile pass accuracy and 88th-percentile passes received confirm this is not a player who hides in easy positions. He takes the ball in tight space, opens his body before the touch arrives, and plays forward immediately. The composure is already formed.

onBall.title: Carries through lines at the 95th percentile
onBall.text: He does not carry because there is no one nearby. He carries through contact, through pressure, finding the release at the moment the defensive line compresses. Low centre of gravity, tight control, agile change of direction in constricted corridors. This is the defining quality of the profile and the reason it scales.

onBall.title: Long passing at the 100th percentile
onBall.text: Elite ball range is rare in a 20-year-old box-to-box midfielder. He switches play precisely and attempts diagonal progression that most midfielders his age avoid. The ambition is matched by the accuracy.

onBall.title: Shot assists and xA both in the upper quartile
onBall.text: At 79th and 78th percentile respectively among all Süper Lig midfielders, the creation output is already functioning at a level that tracks toward top-five league contribution. The third assist involvement at 93rd percentile confirms he is operating as a sequence initiator, not just a ball mover.

onBall.title: Accelerates into transition faster than his peers
onBall.text: 67th percentile in accelerations, but the quality of when he accelerates is what matters: he reads the turnover before it happens and is already moving when possession changes. The transition numbers exceed what the acceleration ranking alone suggests.

offBall.title: Sliding tackles at the 99th percentile
offBall.text: He times the challenge, commits at the right moment, and converts at a rate that puts every other midfielder in the Turkish dataset behind him. The defensive execution is not brute-force pressing. It is precise intervention at the moment the opponent is most vulnerable to it.

offBall.title: Fouls drawn at 86th percentile, fouls committed at 27th
offBall.text: He draws contact regularly and almost never gives it away. That combination describes a player who makes opponents commit to the challenge and then escapes before the infringement. It is also a discipline marker: he does not retaliate, he repositions.

offBall.title: Covers ground across both phases without fading
offBall.text: The pressing contributions are visible in advanced zones, not just when Trabzonspor defends deep. This is a meaningful distinction: he is not a midfielder who hunts the ball defensively when his side is already organised. He presses forward too.

offBall.title: Aerial duels at the 22nd percentile
offBall.text: 1.73m, and the aerial numbers confirm what the frame predicts. This will be targeted directly and consistently at every next level. He compensates through positioning and timing rather than physical authority, but the gap is structural and will not resolve through development.

offBall.title: Defensively disciplined without being a destroyer
offBall.text: Cards at the 9th percentile. He presses, intercepts, and tackles without conceding set pieces. For a midfielder with the aggressive ball-carrying and transition instincts he possesses, this level of defensive cleanliness is unusual. It suggests composure under pressure extends to how he manages physical contests.

pure.name: Ball-carrying through pressure
pure.desc: The 95th-percentile progressive runs figure was already present in Ligue 2 before Trabzonspor gave him a better environment. It is not a product of Trabzonspor's system. The low centre of gravity, close control under contact, and agility in tight corridors are physical and technical habits set well before this season. They transfer because they do not rely on opponents being weaker or spaces being larger.

pure.name: Passing range and accuracy under pressure
pure.desc: 100th percentile in long pass accuracy and 95th in pass accuracy overall at Süper Lig level. The long-range dimension was always there in the profile; Trabzonspor's system and trust gave him the context to use it. Both short and long passing operate as habits of technical confidence, not as context products. They travel.

pure.name: Composure in transition
pure.desc: The cleanliness of his disciplinary record, the fouls drawn vs. fouls conceded gap, the carrying under contact without panicking: these are consistent across both clubs, both leagues, and both roles. Composure is the one trait that almost never disappears when the level rises, because it is a cognitive habit rather than a physical one.

pure.name: Tempo management intelligence
pure.desc: He understands that the ball travels faster than the man and has structured his game around that principle. The carrying is efficient, not compulsive. The passing is progressive but not reckless. The balance between those two things is what separates a midfielder who controls games from one who simply participates in them.

system.name: Chance creation volume
system.desc: The xA and shot assist numbers reflect a player whose final actions now have receivers capable of completing sequences. That is partly context: better teammates, more territorial dominance, more possession in advantageous zones. The vision and execution are his. The volume is partly a function of the environment around him. In a stronger attacking structure, the creative output does not shrink. It expands.

system.name: Sliding tackle volume
system.desc: 99th percentile at Trabzonspor. This is partly system-dependent: Trabzonspor's defensive structure asks him to press aggressively in transition. A deeper defensive role in a possession-dominant system would reduce the sliding tackle volume without affecting the underlying defensive intelligence. The quality of when he commits is the trait. The frequency is the context.

exposed.name: Aerial duels
exposed.desc: 22nd percentile overall, 13th percentile in aerial duels won. At 1.73m this is structural rather than developmental. Every set-piece specialist and physically dominant striker at the next level will find this immediately. He compensates through positioning and timing in ways that partially offset the limitation, but it will not resolve. The question for any buying club is whether their system can protect him from high-ball pressure before he can impose himself on the game through other means.

exposed.name: Duel win rate in ground contests
exposed.desc: 23rd percentile overall despite 99th-percentile sliding tackles. He wins the committed challenge but not the physical 50-50. A top-six Bundesliga or Premier League central midfield will test this regularly. The sliding tackle numbers compensate to a degree, but a midfielder who cannot win ground duels consistently at a high rate carries structural vulnerability in any league that values physical midfield dominance.

athletic.label: Frame
athletic.value: 1.73m, compact and balanced. Low centre of gravity is the decisive physical attribute: enables carrying through contact, rapid direction changes in tight corridors, and agility in duels without relying on size. Will not develop into a physically imposing midfielder. Does not need to.

athletic.label: Explosive pace
athletic.value: Explosive over first meters. The transition acceleration is the most useful expression of his pace: he reads the turnover and is already moving before others have reacted. Elite top-end speed is not the profile. First-step quickness under real pressure is.

athletic.label: Stamina
athletic.value: One of Trabzonspor's most deployed midfielders this season at 20. The engine sustains demanding output across a full campaign. Still needs deliberate conditioning work to handle the cumulative load of top-five league density, where the margins between recovery and fatigue are tighter and the calendar is longer.

athletic.label: Aerial
athletic.value: The structural limitation. 22nd percentile overall. At 1.73m, this does not resolve through development. He compensates with positioning and timing in ways that partially limit the exposure, but every pressing system at the next level will include an aerial challenge plan against him.

athletic.label: Agility
athletic.value: Elite for his position. Changes direction inside tight spaces faster than any midfielder in the Turkish sample. Combined with close control, this is what allows him to carry through pressure rather than around it.

cognitive.label: Tempo control
cognitive.value: He understands that the ball travels faster than the man. This shows in how he plays: the carrying is efficient, not compulsive; the passing is progressive but not reckless; the balance between carry and release is already calibrated. That reading only improves with better teammates and more possession context around him.

cognitive.label: Spatial processing
cognitive.value: Reads the pass before it is needed. The third-assist numbers (93rd percentile) indicate he initiates sequences that require seeing two actions ahead of the ball. That is a cognitive habit, not a creative instinct that varies by match.

cognitive.label: Risk calibration
cognitive.value: Cards at the 9th percentile, fouls at the 27th. He makes aggressive decisions without making reckless ones. That gap between aggression and recklessness is a cognitive quality that defines whether a carrying midfielder is an asset or a liability in high-stakes environments.

cognitive.label: Transition reading
cognitive.value: Converts turnovers into forward momentum almost immediately. The acceleration data is one expression of this. The more important expression is that he positions himself to benefit from turnovers before they happen. That is an attention habit, the hardest cognitive quality to develop at this level.

psychological.label: Pathway
psychological.value: Ivory Coast to academy football in France to the Süper Lig by 19. Each transition required real cultural and professional adjustment made without the safety net of a domestic development system. The composure visible on the pitch reflects that history without needing it stated repeatedly.

psychological.label: Composure under scrutiny
psychological.value: A 20-year-old starting central midfielder at a Süper Lig club with genuine European ambitions. He did not take a peripheral role and grow into it. He took a central one and held it. That is a different thing.

psychological.label: Engagement
psychological.value: The defensive numbers are partly a mentality marker. A 20-year-old midfielder who presses at the 99th percentile for sliding tackles in a league where veterans pick their moments is a player who has not decided that defensive effort is someone else's responsibility. That quality makes him unusually coachable.

psychological.label: Ceiling awareness
psychological.value: No signs of complacency. The profile is still developing, the final-third production is still raw in places, and he continues to take on high-difficulty actions rather than settling into the comfortable role a player of his current value could reasonably occupy.

devPriorities.title: Final-third decision-making and goal threat
devPriorities.text: The creation numbers are already functioning at a high level. The personal output is not yet. 63rd-percentile goals and 24th-percentile touches in the box for a box-to-box midfielder who carries at the 95th percentile is a gap. He arrives at the edge of the final third and delivers for others. The next step is learning when to arrive in the box itself, and when to pull the trigger rather than play the extra pass.

devPriorities.title: Physical conditioning for top-five league density
devPriorities.text: A full season as Trabzonspor's central midfielder is a strong base. But the Premier League, Bundesliga, and Serie A play at a faster tempo with shorter recovery windows across a longer season. His frame needs deliberate strength work to sustain high-intensity output across 50+ matches rather than absorb the occasional game at a less demanding tempo.

devPriorities.title: Managing aerial vulnerability through positioning
devPriorities.text: The structural limitation is not resolvable through development. The management of it is. Specifically: learning to position himself against set pieces in ways that reduce his individual exposure, and to recognize the aerial challenge before it arrives so he can use body position rather than height. This is a coachable habit, not a physical solution.

prose: The profile is ready to leave. The carrying quality, passing range, composure under pressure, and tempo intelligence are formed habits, not developmental projections. They were already present in Ligue 2 before Trabzonspor confirmed them. That progression from conservative environment to competitive environment without regression is the most reliable transferability signal available in scouting.

The 10 reflects the absence of any systemic barrier to a top-five league move. The aerial limitation is real but not a barrier: it is a known and manageable exposure that every serious club will have accounted for before making a bid. The duel win rate is the same. Neither is a structural incompatibility with top-flight football. Both are manageable realities for a player whose other qualities already function at elite levels.

The right immediate next environment is a top-five league club in a possession-dominant system that protects central midfielders from high-ball pressure and rewards progressive carrying and vertical passing. A mid-table Bundesliga or Serie A club with European ambitions fits. So does a pressing team in Ligue 1 that needs a central engine. What would underuse the profile: a direct, aerial-heavy style that makes the limitation a structural weekly problem rather than a managed risk.

leagueScores.note: The physical intensity and press coordination are the main adjustments. His carrying and passing quality already functions at the level required. A mid-table Premier League club with a 6–8 role and a manager who understands possession would extract this profile immediately.

The best fit among the top five. Bundesliga pressing systems with possession-dominant structures reward exactly the profile he offers: carrying through press, vertical passing, transition intelligence, and disciplined defensive contribution. The aerial limitation is more manageable here than in the Premier League.

A natural destination. The progression from Ligue 2 to Süper Lig to Ligue 1 is a coherent arc. The technical and cognitive quality already exceeds what most Ligue 1 clubs require from a central midfielder. The question is whether the club is ambitious enough to use what he brings rather than asking him to play conservatively.

Serie A's tactical sophistication suits the tempo management and passing range. Italian defensive organisation rewards the disciplined carrying and vertical passing. The duel win rate is the main concern in a league that values physical midfield authority. A possession-dominant club would absorb this more cleanly.

La Liga's possession structures and technical emphasis are a natural fit. Spanish clubs at the top level are already scouting midfielders with exactly this profile: technical, progressive, composed under press. The carrying and long passing would become defining assets in the right system.

The maximum-fit environment for this stage of his development if a top-five league club is not yet willing to commit at the price. The Eredivisie would give him high possession volume, progressive structure, and a level of physical management that would develop the final-third dimensions without overwhelming the existing profile.

A strong fit but arguably a step below what the profile now warrants. If Trabzonspor are open to selling to a Belgium club as a stepping stone toward the top five, that is a coherent pathway. If the choice is between Belgium and a direct top-five move, the direct move is the correct recommendation at €17M.

riskGauges.label: Development
riskGauges.note: The core traits are already functioning at a high level in a competitive environment. The development trajectory over the past two seasons is one of the clearest upward progressions in the Turkish dataset. No regression risk is visible.

riskGauges.label: Psychological
riskGauges.note: Ivory Coast to European academy football to Süper Lig starter by 19. Each step required real adjustment. Each step produced a better player. No character concerns from any source.

riskGauges.label: Market
riskGauges.note: €17M with contract to 2030. Value is rising, not plateauing. The risk is not whether Trabzonspor will sell but whether the buying club delays until the price reflects what the profile has already become rather than what it is still becoming.

riskGauges.label: Systemic
riskGauges.note: The profile functions across most modern systems. The one systemic constraint: a direct, aerial-heavy style that places him in regular high-ball exposure before he can impose himself through other means.

players.trajectoryNote: A first-choice central midfielder at a UEFA Champions League club within three to four years.

verdict.statement: Buy the carrying, the passing range, and the composure. They were already present in Ligue 2 before Trabzonspor revealed them, which means they are formed, not circumstantial. The aerial limitation is real and will be managed, not resolved. The duel win rate is the same. Neither is a reason to hesitate at €17M for a 20-year-old who is already controlling games in a top-fifteen European league. The correct move is now, before the market finishes the sentence he has already started writing.
travels: Progressive carries at 95th percentile: trained technique built on low COG and close control, not athleticism over weaker opponents
Long pass accuracy at 100th percentile: already switching play and threading diagonals at elite level
Tempo management intelligence: understood circulation logic in Ligue 2 before the environment rewarded it
Composure under pressure: formed across two leagues, two clubs, two cultures; not a single-context product
Sliding tackle precision at 99th percentile: defensive execution that competes with experienced midfielders twice his age
Creation breadth: shot assists, xA, and 3rd assists all in the top quartile simultaneously
targeted: Aerial duels at 13th percentile: structural, not developmental; requires system protection and positioning discipline
Ground duel win rate at 23rd percentile: will be tested in any physically demanding central midfield role at the next level
Final-third personal output still developing: box presence at 24th percentile for a player carrying at the 95th
verdict.potentialProjection: A first-choice central midfielder at a UEFA Champions League club within three to four years. The carrying and passing range are already functioning at that level in a less demanding environment. What remains is physical development for top-five league density, final-third goal contribution, and one more context step to confirm the profile at full European speed. The ceiling is not speculative. It is already visible in the mechanics of what he does now.

players.verdictLine: Kosovo's pool is not France's pool. That is not an excuse to look away. A 17-year-old producing shots, box touches, progressive runs, and a defensive bite that embarrasses most senior wingers in the same dataset, at a club pushing for European football, is not a coincidence. The mechanism is unfinished. The appetite is not.

description: A natural left-footer operating on the right, cutting inside as the primary mechanism. Already plays as a 10 in some Prishtina phases and has captained Kosovo's U17 side.

The profile does not fit cleanly into a single winger archetype: not yet a dominant 1v1 carrier, not yet a volume creator, not yet a refined passer. What it is instead is a teenage attacker who already finds shots, gets into the box, runs forward, and defends with genuine aggression. A product of the FC 2 Korriku academy, Kosovo's most respected development structure.

onBall.title: The game is already pointed toward goal
onBall.text: Shot volume, box presence, and conversion above expectation together describe a player whose instincts already belong in attacking areas. That kind of forward orientation is harder to teach than technical refinement.

onBall.title: The defensive bite is real
onBall.text: 84th-percentile defensive actions and 87th-percentile sliding tackles for a teenage winger is not a coincidence. He presses forward and tracks back with the same intensity. That quality tends to make coaches trust young players with minutes before their technique justifies it.

onBall.title: He is left-footed on the right
onBall.text: The natural cutting mechanism that produces his best attacking moments is already embedded. It does not need to be taught, only sharpened.

onBall.title: Already playing for Kosovo's U20 side at 16
onBall.text: He was U17 captain. The character markers that come with being trusted with senior and above-age minutes at this early a stage are not nothing.

offBall.title: The dribble success rate is the urgent problem
offBall.text: 10th percentile means that the 1v1 carrying that is central to the profile's identity is failing more than it is succeeding in this league. Better defences compress faster. This needs to resolve before the next step makes sense.

offBall.title: Head position during carries
offBall.text: Multiple observations flag that he loses sight of teammates during dribbles. The vision and the carrying are not yet connected. When they are, the creative numbers will move.

offBall.title: The final ball weight and timing
offBall.text: Whether crossing from wide or playing through balls, the execution is uneven. The intent is clear; the delivery does not always match it.

offBall.title: Physical robustness
offBall.text: Not the strongest in aerial or ground duels. At 181cm the frame is there to grow into. But the current duel numbers reflect a player who competes without yet dominating.

pure.name: Attacking instinct and vertical game orientation
pure.desc: The game moves toward goal when he has it. He reads momentum and attacks it rather than waiting for structure to open. This is not a tactical instruction or a product of a particular system. It is a cognitive habit already embedded at 17. That kind of orientation does not need to be taught. It needs to be organised.

pure.name: Bravery in possession
pure.desc: Receives responsibility without flinching. Takes on defenders repeatedly. Shoots with conviction. Attempts difficult actions. These are habits of psychological confidence that tend to arrive early in players or not at all. Coaching can sharpen them but rarely installs them from scratch.

pure.name: Defensive engagement
pure.desc: 84th-percentile defensive actions and 87th-percentile sliding tackles for a 17-year-old inside forward are not instructed behaviours. They reflect a player whose competitive instinct extends to defending. This does not disappear when the level rises. It usually becomes a significant organisational asset.

context.name: Selective crossing quality
context.desc: 98th-percentile cross accuracy on 36th-percentile volume. Whether this reflects timing intelligence or avoidance is unresolved. In a system that actively asks him to deliver from wide, the quality is genuinely impressive. In one that does not, the sample stays too thin to draw firm conclusions.

system.name: Goal conversion
system.desc: 72nd-percentile conversion in the Kosovo Superliga at 17. This needs a significant context discount. The defence quality and pressing intensity in this league bear no resemblance to where the profile needs to function eventually. The instinct to finish is real. Whether the efficiency holds at a meaningful next step is a different and much harder question.

exposed.name: Dribble success rate
exposed.desc: 10th percentile. The most structurally significant limitation in the profile right now. Any defence at the next level will identify the carry as the primary attacking mechanism and defend it tightly. If the efficiency does not improve, the profile becomes predictable before it becomes dangerous.

exposed.name: Creative output and vision in possession
exposed.desc: xA at 19th, shot assists at 30th. The head-down tendency during carries means he is not yet converting attacking situations into outcomes for teammates. Any coach preparing to face him will simply ensure someone tracks his run while leaving the rest of the attack uncovered. Until he lifts his head, that exposure is manageable.

athletic.label: Frame
athletic.value: 181cm, slender but with a strong lower body. Low centre of gravity is the defining physical quality: allows him to manoeuvre through tight spaces and shield the ball despite not being physically imposing. Still filling out.

athletic.label: Pace
athletic.value: Explosive over short distances. Uses the first step well in transition. Not a pure pace winger but quick enough to create separation when the movement is timed correctly.

athletic.label: Two-footedness
athletic.value: Natural left-footer, notably comfortable on his right. The ambipedal quality expands the threat profile beyond the predictable inside cut and makes him harder to position against.

athletic.label: Aerial
athletic.value: 17th percentile in aerial duel win rate. At 181cm this is an application problem, not a structural one. He is not using his height. This is coachable but requires deliberate work.

athletic.label: Shot power
athletic.value: Noted as impressive for his age. The 82nd-percentile shot volume and above-expected conversion confirm this is a genuine physical attribute, not just aggressive shot selection.

cognitive.label: Game orientation
cognitive.value: Vertical by instinct. The game moves toward goal when he has it. This is not a player who circulates safely until structure opens. He reads momentum and attacks it. That instinct is already rare at his age.

cognitive.label: Transition reading
cognitive.value: Times runs behind defences well. Already getting into the right areas before the pass arrives, which is a cognitive marker that tends to improve with experience rather than regress.

cognitive.label: Loose ball awareness
cognitive.value: Sharp to second balls and loose contacts. This contributes to both the shot volume and the defensive action numbers. He is always looking for the next action.

cognitive.label: Vision in possession
cognitive.value: The gap in the profile. Loses sight of teammates during dribbles. The awareness of space around him is ahead of the awareness of players in that space. A development priority, not a ceiling.

psychological.label: Fearlessness
psychological.value: The standout quality. Takes players on in 1v1 situations without hesitation. Shoots with conviction. Attempts bold actions. None of the tentativeness that usually marks teenage attackers playing senior football for the first time.

psychological.label: Leadership signals
psychological.value: Kosovo U17 captain. Playing for the U20 side at 16. These are markers of a player who is trusted under pressure in competitive contexts, not just in comfort situations.

psychological.label: Competitive context
psychological.value: Scored in a key European fixture against Sheriff Tiraspol. That is a specific high-pressure moment that produces either confidence or exposure. The fact that it produced a goal matters.

psychological.label: Kosovar identity
psychological.value: Widely regarded as the most promising young attacker in Kosovo right now. In a small footballing nation, that carries weight as a development context: everyone knows who you are and what is expected of you from a very early age.

devPriorities.title: Dribble efficiency and head position during carries
devPriorities.text: These are the same problem expressed differently. The technical execution of the carry needs to improve, but the more fundamental fix is learning to carry with his head up so that the decision after the carry is available before the defender has committed. Once the vision and the carrying are connected, the creative numbers will follow almost automatically.

devPriorities.title: Final ball weight and timing
devPriorities.text: The technical imagination is already ahead of the execution. He sees the right pass. The delivery does not always arrive with the right weight or at the right moment. Deliberate repetition of crossing, through-ball, and cut-back delivery under match-speed conditions is the specific work. This is a repetition problem, not a vision problem.

devPriorities.title: Physical robustness and aerial application
devPriorities.text: The frame at 181cm is there to grow into. Senior defenders will target the aerial ball and the physical contest deliberately. Strength work over the next two seasons and specific aerial challenge practice will address both. Neither is a structural ceiling. Both are currently active limitations at the next level.

devPriorities.title: Exposure to a better competitive environment
devPriorities.text: The Kosovo Superliga is not the context that resolves the development questions this profile raises. A step toward a developing Balkan or Central European league, or a strong youth competition at a higher-level club, would compress the timeline for understanding whether the tape-level qualities genuinely scale.

prose: The 4 reflects a specific and honest position: the attacking instinct, defensive engagement, and psychological confidence are qualities that belong at a higher level than Kosovo football. They are not products of a favourable context. They are already present in the playing style itself.

But the dribble success, the creative output, and the physical robustness are all genuinely limiting at this moment. A step directly to a top-five league or even a competitive mid-tier league would expose those gaps before the profile has had the environment to close them. The right next step is a developmental context, not a performance context.

The Jupiler Pro League or a mid-table Eredivisie club represents the highest credible immediate next environment. Anything above that asks the physical and technical profile to be something it is not yet. A developmental move within the Balkans or Central Europe in the near term would be the right step before that conversation becomes relevant.

leagueScores.note: Not yet. Not close. The physical and technical gaps that are manageable in Kosovo become structural problems in the Premier League.
The pressing intensity and technical standard are too demanding for the current profile. A future destination if the development arc follows the potential.
Italian defensive organisation would neutralise the current dribbling profile quickly. The tactical and technical requirements exceed what is currently available.
A significant stretch at this stage. The technical standard is demanding. Possible as a medium-term step after a developmental intermediate move.
The ceiling of current readiness. Physically manageable, technically demanding enough to develop the profile, and a league with a track record of accelerating young inside forwards.

The most appropriate immediate next environment. A mid-table Albanian, North Macedonian, Serbian, or Croatian club that demands more technically than Kosovo without overwhelming the physical development timeline.

riskGauges.label: Development
riskGauges.note: The dribble success and creative output are genuine gaps. Whether they resolve with development is the central unknown. Technical habits at 17 are not yet fully formed, which cuts both ways.

riskGauges.label: Psychological
riskGauges.note: U17 captain, U20 inclusion at 16, European goal at 17. The character indicators are already strong and have been tested in environments above age expectation.

riskGauges.label: Market
riskGauges.note: €200k with no contract end date and no agent. Acquirable for developmental purposes without competitive pressure. The acquisition cost is negligible relative to the ceiling if development follows.

riskGauges.label: Systemic
riskGauges.note: The profile requires a system that gives him freedom to carry and cut inside while developing the technical output gaps. Drop him into a rigid defensive structure or a physical midfield battle and the profile disappears.

players.trajectoryNote: A ceiling of a first-choice inside forward at a top-flight European club is plausible if the dribbling and creative output develop to match the attacking instincts already present. This is the most speculative potential rating in the database.

verdict.statement: This is a watch-list report, not a transfer recommendation. The data establishes a teenager with genuine attacking instinct, unusual defensive engagement, and a profoundly unresolved technical question at the centre of the profile. The dribble success needs the tape. The creative output needs a stronger environment to distinguish talent from context. Track him through the summer. If a developmental move materialises, revisit. If he stays in Prishtina for another season, the next dataset will be far more instructive than this one.
travels: Shot volume at 82nd percentile and goal conversion at 72nd: the scoring instinct is already functioning above expectation
Defensive actions at 84th, sliding tackles at 87th: the competitive engagement is unconditional and already stands out among senior peers
Left-footed on the right with genuine right-foot capability: the cutting mechanism is embedded
Cross accuracy at 98th percentile on selective delivery: possible hidden strength worth tracking
U17 captain, U20 inclusion at 16, European goal against Sheriff Tiraspol: character markers that matter
targeted: Dribble success at 10th percentile: the core carrying mechanism is currently failing more than succeeding
xA at 19th, shot assists at 30th: the appetite to score is not yet extending to creating for others
Head position during carries: vision and carrying not yet connected; limits decision quality when in possession
Kosovo Superliga discount: the context requires sustained re-evaluation before transfer action is justified
verdict.potentialProjection: A ceiling of a first-choice inside forward at a top-flight European club is plausible if the dribbling and creative output develop to match the attacking instincts already present. This is the most speculative potential rating in the database. It reflects what the appetite, the defensive engagement, and the attacking instinct suggest is possible, not what the current mechanism guarantees.

players.verdictLine: Teenage centre-backs are supposed to be incomplete. Nikola Simić is not. The statistical profile is unusually complete. The question is no longer whether he becomes a professional defender. It's how high his ceiling goes and whether he is a future top-level centre-back or simply a very good one.

description: A left-sided centre-back who plays like the position is a creative role as much as a defensive one. Participates in build-up with genuine passing range, carries into midfield when the moment arrives, and wins his defensive duels with composure rather than aggression.

The profile is most naturally a left CB in a back four or back three, with license to step into midfield under pressure. Ambidextrous and physically imposing at 192cm, his natural profile fits possession-dominant systems with a high defensive line and genuine build-up demand from centre-backs.

onBall.title: 96th-percentile pass accuracy, 88th-percentile passes per 90
onBall.text: The passing volume is high and the quality is elite. He is not a safe recycler hiding from the ball. He receives often, in tight areas, and almost never gives it away. The short-medium accuracy and the long ball accuracy both sit in the elite band together, which is the combination that tells you the range is genuine rather than selective.

onBall.title: CIES ranked first globally among U20 CBs for opponents bypassed per 90
onBall.text: Across 46 top domestic divisions. This is not a Serbian Super Liga number; it is a global number. The mechanism that produces it is carrying through press and playing line-breaking forward passes at elite volume and accuracy.

onBall.title: 95th-percentile dribble success rate
onBall.text: For a centre-back, this is the profile's most unusual data point. It hints at composure rather than athleticism: he does not dribble to escape danger, he dribbles because he has already assessed the situation and committed to a controlled exit. Young CBs who complete dribbles at this rate are comfortable manipulating pressure before it arrives.

onBall.title: Forward passes at 94th percentile, passes into final third at 90th
onBall.text: He plays early and plays forward. The build-up contribution is not just circulation. He is consistently finding the pass that breaks lines or advances territory. That is the technical and cognitive quality that makes him a genuine possession asset rather than a ball-secure recycler.

onBall.title: Steps into midfield when the structure invites it
onBall.text: Not a compulsive carrier but a selective one. When the line opens, he commits to the advance and plays the next pass from there. The positional reading that governs those moments is already well above what the age would predict.

offBall.title: 93rd-percentile duels won, 90th-percentile defensive duel win rate
offBall.text: Wins his individual contests at an elite rate. The mechanism is composure and positioning rather than aggression: he waits for the right moment, stays on his feet, and forces the attacker into a losing position before committing to the challenge. Almost never gets beaten by the first touch.

offBall.title: Defensive action volume is modest at the 7th percentile
offBall.text: Low defensive action volume at Partizan does not mean passive defending. It means the team concedes few dangerous positions, and when they do, Simić has usually already cut the situation off before it fully developed. Positioning prevents the action from being required.

offBall.title: 74th-percentile aerial duel win rate, 65th-percentile aerial duels won per 90
offBall.text: Good rather than dominant aerially. At 192cm the volume and win rate should both be higher eventually. The quality in contested moments is there. The assertiveness in seeking out aerial contact is the development question at the next level.

offBall.title: Covers channels with intelligent positioning rather than reactive recovery
offBall.text: He reads runs early, positions his body to cut the angle before the ball is played, and rarely needs the sprint recovery because the problem was already solved positionally. The recovery pace is there when needed; the positioning means it rarely is.

offBall.title: Cards at the 92nd percentile and fouls at the 86th
offBall.text: He is not a dirty player. He is a player who competes for the ball rather than hanging back. The discipline within those contests is high: the fouls are contested ground duels, not late challenges or cynical interventions. It is an engagement marker, not a conduct concern.

pure.name: Composure under press
pure.desc: 96th-percentile pass accuracy at Partizan, where the opposition quality and pressing demand are the highest in Serbian domestic football. He is not being protected by a soft league context. The composure is tested regularly. It holds regularly. That is the definition of a trait that travels.

pure.name: Duel winning: ground and aerial
pure.desc: 93rd percentile overall duels, 90th defensive duels. The mechanism is positioning and timing rather than physical assertion alone. Those cognitive qualities travel unconditionally. The physical component will strengthen as he matures, which means the ceiling on this trait is still rising.

pure.name: Vertical passing ambition and accuracy
pure.desc: 94th-percentile forward passes. Not just volume but precision and intent. He attempts the line-breaking pass when it is available and converts it at a rate that confirms the decision is correct more often than not. In any possession system at any level, this is a defining asset.

pure.name: Ambidexterity
pure.desc: Genuinely two-footed. No defensive teams can force him onto his weaker side because there is no weak side to exploit. At the highest level this matters enormously: it expands the passing angles available, removes a predictable pressing trigger, and means he can play both sides of a back four or back three without tactical compromise.

system.name: Defensive action volume
system.desc: The 7th-percentile defensive actions reflect Partizan's dominance in Serbian domestic football rather than passive defending. In a more contested environment where the team does not monopolise territory, the volume will rise. The underlying defensive quality is already present; the context suppresses the number.

system.name: Proactiveness vs. stabiliser balance
system.desc: Currently prioritises control and stability over assertive stepping. In a system with a very high line that demands centre-backs to step aggressively and win the ball forward, that calibration will need to shift. The quality for it exists. Whether it becomes a consistent habit is the interesting developmental question.

exposed.name: Aerial assertiveness
exposed.desc: 74th percentile win rate at 192cm is good but not dominant. Set-piece specialists and aerial forwards at the next level will test whether the quality holds when the physical challenge is more sustained. The frame is clearly sufficient. The assertiveness in seeking out aerial contact needs to match it.

exposed.name: Pressing resilience under sustained high tempo
exposed.desc: Multiple observers note he has benefited from not being pressed intensely in domestic football. The composure when pressed is described as decent rather than elite. In the Bundesliga or Premier League, where every build-up phase involves coordinated press, this will be stress-tested immediately and regularly.

athletic.label: Frame
athletic.value: 192cm, still developing physically. Already imposing for ground duels and commanding in central areas. The frame will continue to fill out over the next two to three years, which means the physical ceiling of the profile has not yet been reached.

athletic.label: Pace
athletic.value: Quick enough for recovery at this level. Not an elite sprinter. Compensates intelligently with positioning: he rarely needs to recover at full pace because the positional reading means he is rarely caught wrong.

athletic.label: Strength
athletic.value: Strong in ground duels. Wins physical contests through body positioning and leverage rather than just size. The 93rd-percentile duel win rate reflects a player who already understands how to use his frame, not just one who is larger than his opponents.

athletic.label: Agility
athletic.value: Good footwork for his size. The 95th-percentile dribble success and the descriptions of clean technical manipulation in tight spaces confirm that the agility in restricted areas is genuine. Does not look like a 192cm player in those moments.

athletic.label: Aerial
athletic.value: 74th-percentile win rate. Good but not yet dominant for the frame. The ceiling here is clearly higher than current numbers suggest. Physical maturity and deliberate work on aerial assertiveness will close the gap.

cognitive.label: Spatial reading
cognitive.value: The defining quality. He reads runs early, positions himself to cut angles before the ball is played, and manages his zone without needing to react to danger after it develops. The low defensive action volume is partly a product of this: he is rarely reactive because he is rarely late.

cognitive.label: Decision speed in possession
cognitive.value: Plays early and plays forward. The elite forward pass and pass accuracy numbers together confirm that the decision of when to play and where to play is consistently correct. He does not hold the ball under pressure. He finds the right action before the press arrives.

cognitive.label: Duel timing
cognitive.value: Waits for the right moment before engaging rather than lunging at the first opportunity. He forces the attacker to commit first, then wins the contest from a better position. This is what produces 93rd-percentile duel wins without high foul counts.

cognitive.label: Proactive vs. reactive balance
cognitive.value: Currently more stabiliser than aggressor. Defends with calmness and structure rather than assertive stepping. Whether this develops into a more front-foot defensive profile is the open projection question. The qualities for both exist. The default is still conservative.

psychological.label: Responsibility accepted early
psychological.value: Regular starter at Partizan at 19. The most demanding club in Serbian football does not give a teenage CB nearly 3,000 minutes without genuine trust in the profile. He has not just survived that responsibility. He has become one of the club's most deployed defenders.

psychological.label: Composure under scrutiny
psychological.value: Serbia U19 international with 14 caps and a goal. The national team pathway is accelerating at the same time as the club demand is rising. No signs of a player shrinking under the weight of expectation.

psychological.label: Maturity of approach
psychological.value: Multiple observers describe him as playing beyond his years. The composure, the positioning discipline, the patience in duels: these are habits usually associated with senior players in their mid-twenties, not teenagers.

psychological.label: Leadership signals
psychological.value: Recognised as a central figure in Partizan's defensive structure rather than a rotation option. Worth watching whether the leadership formalises as his senior club profile continues to grow.

devPriorities.title: Aerial assertiveness and volume
devPriorities.text: The quality in contested aerial moments is already there. What needs to develop is the instinct to seek those moments out rather than allowing them to arrive. A 192cm centre-back who actively attacks aerial balls is one of the most physically imposing defensive presences in the game. He is not yet that player. He can become it with deliberate work on aerial aggression and jump timing.

devPriorities.title: Pressing resilience under sustained coordinated pressure
devPriorities.text: The composure when pressed has been described as decent rather than elite. Top-five league build-up phases involve continuous, coordinated pressing without the recovery windows Serbian domestic football provides. Accumulated exposure to high-press systems will sharpen this. It is not a technical problem. It is a tempo and reading problem that repetition resolves.

devPriorities.title: Developing a more proactive stepping profile
devPriorities.text: Currently defends with composure and stability. The qualities to become more assertive in stepping to win the ball forward are already present in the duel numbers. A system that specifically asks him to step and disrupt in transition would accelerate this dimension. It is a contextual development question, not a ceiling one.

prose: The composure, the passing range, the duel quality, and the positional intelligence are already functioning at a level that belongs in the top-five leagues. They are not products of a weak domestic league. They are tested at Partizan, which is a genuine European-level club environment in terms of demand and scrutiny.

The 8 reflects two honest gaps rather than fundamental barriers. First, the aerial assertiveness question: the win rate is good, but a top-five league will test the volume commitment more aggressively than Serbian football has. Second, the pressing resilience under sustained coordinated press: it has not been stress-tested at the frequency a top-five league demands. Both are developmental gaps, not structural incompatibilities.

The Bundesliga is the natural destination for this profile. The technical and positional demands suit the passing quality and duel composure. A mid-table Bundesliga club with a possession-dominant system would extract the profile immediately. Serie A or La Liga offer similar structural fits. The Premier League's physicality is manageable given the frame; the immediate pressing intensity is the adaptation challenge.

leagueScores.note: The physical adjustment is manageable given the frame. The pressing intensity and tempo are the genuine challenge. A season at a mid-table Premier League club with a possession-minded manager is a credible near-term destination.

The best immediate fit. Bundesliga possession systems reward exactly this profile: composure under press, vertical passing range, duel quality, and the intelligence to manage zones rather than just win physical contests. A natural destination.

A strong fit with the technical demands. French domestic football has produced several CBs with similar profiles who went on to top-five careers. The physical challenge is somewhat less intense than the Premier League or Bundesliga.

Italian tactical sophistication suits the positional intelligence and composure under press. Serie A's defensive organisation is built around exactly the zonal reading and duel timing he already demonstrates. A possession-dominant club would extract the full profile.

The technical and possession demands of La Liga suit the profile exceptionally well. Spanish clubs actively recruit CBs who can bypass press and play forward accurately. The profile fits naturally into the tactical demands at the top end of the table.

Maximum readiness. Dutch football rewards the complete technical and positional profile he already offers. A season in the Eredivisie before a top-five move would sharpen the pressing resilience question and add another layer of European credibility.
